characters.name: Lieutenant Christopher Thompkins
characters.bio: Main protagonist and leader of Team Falcon. Twin to Josh — a scar on his upper right lip from a childhood zipline accident is the only distinguishing facial feature between them. A decorated soldier who graduated high school in 2046. Born in south Texas, raised in eastern Idaho. Father Trey served twenty years in the Navy before dying in a car accident. Mother Julie was a molecular engineer at BioSteel Cybernetic Industries. Determined, strategic, and emotionally grounded by his relationships with the crew.

versions.bio: Main protagonist and leader of Team Falcon. Twin to Josh — a scar on his upper right lip from a childhood zipline accident is the only distinguishing facial feature between them. A decorated soldier who graduated high school in 2046. Born in south Texas, raised in eastern Idaho. Father Trey served twenty years in the Navy before dying in a car accident. Mother Julie was a molecular engineer at BioSteel Cybernetic Industries. Known for exceptional close-quarters combat skills and designated BMF by his CO Lieutenant Greene after every successful mission. Determined, strategic, and emotionally grounded by his relationships with the crew.

Main protagonist and leader of Team Falcon. After being mauled by a Garello and left near death, Chris lost both legs below the knee and his right arm. Josh replaced them with cybernetic parts integrated directly to nerve and bone via nanites — a design that in one respect surpasses even Aku'Ra cybernetics. Bonded with the Aku'Ra AI Liam, who lives within his mind. Now part man, part machine — the Svixen call him Dova Sku: Hell Machine.

storyEvents.event: Leads Team Falcon through a combat training simulation aboard the Light Skipper. Uses a paintex grenade to scatter the enemy team and orders Bravo Squad to fire. Wins the engagement but loses five simulated soldiers including Miller and Vasquez. Gathers the team for a debrief in Command Central, interacting with Miller, Patel, Robertson, Linkenbach, and Zavani.

At home in Idaho, cleaning his PMG M4 H2 while Josh works nearby. The chapter reveals their shared backstory — father Trey's death in a car accident, mother Julie's work at BioSteel, Josh's invention of PMG at 15, and Chris's Navy career. The brothers have not seen each other in seven years.

Returns home on leave in April 2053. The Catoran discovery is announced three days into his visit. He raises the mission idea at dinner that night. Julie encourages them both to go — then dies two weeks later from a brain tumor she had hidden from them. The boys find her letter the next day. Three weeks after the funeral, Chris convinces Josh to go, revealing he has already submitted both their applications. Both are accepted within 48 hours.

Group photo at Spaceport America on December 21. Chris and Josh board the Light Skipper and part on Deck Two with a fist bump. After checking in with Captain Terzi and entering final notes, he climbs into his cryo pod, magnetically attaches the family photo to the inside lid, says goodbye to his parents, and enters cryosleep.

Wakes from two years of cryosleep with severe nausea. His pod neighbor turns out to be Kat Andersen. They exchange jokes about the cryo fluid and their scars — she points to his collarbone scar, he responds by showing the one on his buttock. He heads to the bridge where Captain Terzi briefs him: first planetfall mission at 0800, Team Falcon only, four hours to recon and report back.

Leads Falcon Team One in the first human planetary landing on Catora. Discovers the mud frog alongside Davis — attempts and fails to catch one. Witnesses a Laredo dragon attack on Demolitions Tech Chung. Observes dozens of glowing Skamp eyes watching them from the tree line at dusk.

Oversees the construction of the FOB using LSPD-printed perimeter walls. When a Laredo dragon attacks engineers sheltering in a storage container, he orders incendiary rounds and approves thermal HUD filter upgrades after standard kinetic rounds fail to penetrate the animal's hide.

Accompanies the Skamp expedition to the inland lake. Witnesses the Laredo dragon egg incident and the injury of Theo Bronx. Captain Terzi institutes the Catoran Fixed Calendar — a 389-day calendar with a 13th month called Galus.

Follows a magnetic anomaly with Falcon Two to an underground Sepu hydroelectric cave. Discovers the first obelisk in a hexagonal cavern. Kirk Thorne names it 'The Chapel.' Research Base Alpha is established around the cave entrance.

A second, 50-meter obelisk is found at a new site — hollow inside, with a false door and an alien datapad displaying English text. Research Base Bravo is established. A garello ambushes the team. Chris loses both legs above the knee and his right arm at the shoulder. Robertson and Linkenbach fell a tree to kill it. Linkenbach uses Chris's suit battery to open the obelisk door for medical access, inadvertently preventing Chris from sealing his wounds sooner.

Wakes from a coma to a voice inside his mind — the Aku'Ra AI LM2479623, whose matrix transferred into his bloodstream through the obelisk nanites during the attack. Josh has already installed cybernetic legs and a cybernetic right arm integrated directly to nerve and bone. Chris names the AI 'Liam.'

Recovers physically while Liam shares history of the Aku'Ra, the Sepu, and the Svixen. Josh presents the Drake Scale exosuit — matte black, overlapping PMG plates, 'Josh was here' inscribed inside the cover. Chris learns for the first time the scale of what they have walked into.

Liam installs a wireless transducer inside Chris's cybernetic arm — an extremely painful 27-minute procedure. Chris can now mentally control nearby wireless devices. Tests Sepu tarlon commands through his suit's external speakers: the alpha of a nearby pack retreats on the 'retreat' command.

Liam tells Chris the full history of the Millennium War — the Svixen's arrival at Su'Laeris, the thousand-year conflict, the Hardlight Shield turning point, and the Skrema duel between Praetor Turfish and Chapaka Flacos Pointan that ended it and drove the Svixen from the system.

Escorts Josh on a three-site Sepu ruins expedition. Works with Josh and Liam to design Liam's avatar — a humanoid figure blending Aku'Ra and human features. Josh installs the shoulder holoprojector. Liam's avatar debuts publicly, projected in front of Chris's shoulder for the first time.

Liam reveals Earth's alien history to the assembled crew via his new avatar: the Aku'Ra uplift, the Svixen invasion and the Younger Dryas event, the Tower of Babel and Noah's Ark as in-universe historical echoes. Chris processes the revelation that humanity's ancient history is not what it believed.

Oversees Kirk Thorne's reactivation of the Sepu hydroelectric facility. A third buried Transync Station is found. Chris tests tarlon commands on a pack encountered during a fapu expedition — the alpha responds to the 'retreat' command.

A Regulus-class Svixen battleship is detected on approach. Chris leads the full crew evacuation from the FOB to the abandoned mining cave 1,920km northeast. Organizes the defensive perimeter and keeps the crew hidden and calm.

During one of his daily hilltop monitoring trips, a tarlon with a distinctive white shoulder scar approaches and sits beside him. Chris uses Sepu commands — the tarlon responds. After fifteen minutes sitting together at the hilltop, he coaxes it inside the cave using the 'come' command, ordering the crew not to shoot. Recognizes it as the same tarlon his team freed from debris during an earlier expedition.

Names the tarlon Fenrir after the Norse mythological wolf. The Svixen ship moves out of sight. Contact with the Light Skipper is restored. Captain Terzi gives him rendezvous coordinates — the Light Skipper will EM-tether beyond the moon at precisely 2400 hours. Chris announces the evacuation and coordinates the load-out. Fenrir refuses the 'stay' order and waits near the cave entrance as LOLA departs.

Back aboard the Light Skipper. The Svixen attack cripples the engines. Three Svixen space tug craft attach to the hull and redirect the ship toward Catora for a crash. Chris works with Captain Terzi and Liam to plan the escape. Orders Team Falcon to the cargo bays and boards a mining vessel as Razor's co-pilot. Liam remotely controls the docking systems to coordinate the evacuation.

The mining vessels crash on Catora. Chris leads his group toward Sepu ruins. Huffman is killed in a Sveri Sku ambush while Davis's team is moving. Davis makes contact. Linkenbach proposes using the old mining cave hideout as a defensible fallback — Chris agrees and coordinates LOLA One supply flights through Captain Terzi.

Battle at the mining cave. A Sveri Sku suicide bomber destroys the perimeter wall. Nunez is killed. Chris is knocked unconscious by a Sveri Sku blow to his helmet — Liam briefly takes control of his body to fight back and boost him with adrenaline. Coming around, Chris mentally connects to every wireless device in the cave and shouts 'SKREMA!' through every speaker at maximum volume.

Steps out of the Drake Scale suit to face Svixen Chapaka Semula Gatuum in Skrema. Introduces himself: 'Lieutenant Christopher Thompkins of the United States Navy and Earth's Galactic Navy. Son of Trey and Julie and brother to Josh.' Wins the duel by chokehold. Gatuum breaks the code by grabbing a rifle — Fenrir appears from the bushes and tears out his throat. Semula Breven appears, confirms 1,000 years of peace for Catora, and explains the Dova Sku title before departing.

Searches the cave for survivors. Finds Morgan with a broken leg and infected arm, and Carmelo buried under rubble. Searches LOLA Two's cockpit and finds Josh, Kat, Rowley, Marie, and Lin alive. Embraces Josh. Reflects on the family photo in his suit's wrist cover.

Walks four days alone to the FOB to retrieve medical supplies for Morgan, with Fenrir trotting alongside. Finds a cache of medical supplies, inert nanites, and PMG plates in Josh's room. Rights an overturned UTV and starts the drive back. Fenrir refuses to ride in the UTV bed — hops out the moment Chris starts moving and runs alongside instead.

Returns to the cave hideout with the UTV loaded with supplies and nanites. Liam uses nanites externally for the first time, healing Morgan's infected arm by pressing through Chris's palm. Oversees the cremation ceremony for the 234 fallen crewmembers.

Scouts the Light Skipper crash site for usable salvage. All 15 survivors receive medically-programmed nanites distributed by Josh and activated by Liam. Chris receives an upgraded nanite package. Carmelo formally defers ground command to Chris while retaining ship authority.

Research Base Bravo is restored as permanent headquarters. Crew meeting held. Chris proposes the trantion reactivation mission — he and a physicist will take LOLA One to the Lagrange Points. Josh formally proposes building a physical body for Liam. Chris gives his approval.

Departs on LOLA One with Dr. Rowley for the 35-day trantion mission. A Svixen scout ship is detected monitoring them — Liam plants six missiles in its flight path and detonates them as it passes, destroying it. Rowley develops severe space sickness; Chris solves it by suggesting he stand at the rear ramp during engine burns.

Both trantions are reactivated — the second requires a spacewalk to deliver nanites for repair. The moment both come online, the Transync Station at Research Base Bravo activates. Five crew members (Josh, Carmelo, Kat, Linkenbach, Davis) are immediately abducted through the portal by Maaket soldiers.
Returns to Catora with Rowley. Learns five are missing after reviewing Liam's sensor footage. Identifies the portal destination as Tual from Liam's Aku'Ra records. Assembles a rescue team.

Leads a team through the Transync portal to Tual. Inside the Maaket station, the team is surrounded by guards. Chris assesses the odds and chooses surrender over a fight, allowing themselves to be taken to the palace.

Held in a Maaket cell with a false-image barrier. Brought before Princess Cashy, who suspects the crew of involvement in her parents' assassination. Chris clears them of suspicion. The crew is released. Cautious negotiations begin.

Negotiates the release of the abducted crew. Josh agrees to remain on Tual voluntarily to work with Maaket engineers on new technology. The rest of the crew returns to Catora through the Transync portal.

Arrives at Seutor through the Calores Transync Station. The city is under attack from Natuul/Svixen forces. The team is ambushed in the streets and retreats into Tuaca underground tunnels. Meets Patron Rohga, who offers a deal: deliver a sealed pindemo to Guardian Phostop at Lesfer in exchange for a letter of passage and two guides.

Team rests overnight in the Tuaca tunnel network. Rohga provides the satchel containing the pindemo and a letter of introduction. Posis and Tivay officially accept the guide role despite their earlier failed detention mission.

Trek through Seutor's bioluminescent jungle. A Vyasin attacks Tivay — Chris kills it by driving a suit-formed blade through the joint in its neck carapace. Liam pushes nanites through Chris's hand into Tivay's bloodstream, saving him from fatal injuries. Posis's hostility toward the humans dissolves.

Arrives at the canyon city of Lesfer. Meets Guardian Phostop. Delivers the pindemo. Phostop is immediately suspicious of how Liam knew about the incoming missile strike before Tuaca sensors detected it. Natuul/Svixen missiles bombard the city. Liam takes control of all four exosuits simultaneously for approximately 12 seconds, moving the entire team 200 meters to safety.

Addresses the aftermath of Liam's suit takeover with Phostop. Liam privately tells Chris the experience was 'thrilling' — which unsettles both of them. Svixen ground troops begin entering Lesfer. Chris breaks out of the Tuaca command center and shouts Skrema, issuing the challenge.

Fights Skrema against Tagonis Riveen, Warlord of Clan Lawtem. Riveen wields a plasma-edged scimitar. Chris wins using combat instincts guided by Liam's real-time heart-location overlay. Takes Riveen's kopesh from a dead Svixen soldier and uses it to deliver the killing blow. Riveen dies on his feet calling Chris Dova Sku with what appears to be genuine respect.
Phostop's suspicion is replaced by respect after the Skrema victory. The crew is awarded a Ground Runner long-distance transport ship as payment for saving Seutor. Prepares to return to Catora.

Returns to Research Base Bravo through the Transync portal with Posis and Tivay. Reunites with the remaining crew. Robertson starts chanting 'Hellmachine, Hellmachine' when the Seutor story is told. Plans are made for a full crew transit back to Seutor to retrieve the ship.

On Seutor at Lesfer, leading the hangar debris-clearing operation. Makes structural decisions using Liam's guidance. Decides to go to Tual to retrieve Josh before committing to departure. Assigns Carmelo to lead the dig in his absence.

On Tual. Retrieves Josh from the Maaket underground lab — finds him unharmed, surrounded by scientists, and wearing a grease-stained grin. Josh has built a Maaket-version Drake Scale suit for Ankir. During the audience with Princess Cashy, Ankir requests permission to join Team Falcon as a volunteer fighter. Cashy grants it. Chris accepts Ankir. Returns to Seutor.

Returns from Tual to Lesfer. Kat, Lemmon, and Campbell each announce they are choosing to stay behind on Seutor. Chris accepts all three decisions. The Ascendant's systems activate corridor by corridor. Chris steps back from the captain's chair and gives it formally to Carmelo. The Ascendant fires its first thruster test.

Final preparations and farewells to Kat, Lemmon, and Campbell. Kat kisses his cheek and tells him to make the Svixen regret the Dova Sku name. Liam gives Campbell a submind on a data chip to help fly LOLA. Chris boards the Ascendant last. Liam is formally introduced to Ankir, Tivay, and Posis. The Ascendant launches from Lesfer.

In space ascending from Seutor's system, a Va'korrin-class Svixen carrier is detected closing fast. Battle stations. The ship takes plasma hits. Josh deploys the resonance inverter for the first time. Svixen boarders breach the hull. Chris retrieves his kopesh from his cabin and moves to repel boarders with Ankir.

Repels the first boarding party. Josh's energy-disrupting orb neutralizes Svixen weapons at close range. Miller is mortally wounded saving Blevins. Chris presses his hand to Miller's wound and Liam forces nanites in — a last effort. A Svixen commander is captured. When Morgan reports Miller has died, Chris draws his sidearm and executes the Svixen commander without ceremony. Orders the recording sent to the enemy carrier.

Second boarding action from a dropship that slipped sensors. Ankir saves Morgan from a surprise attack using his cloaking suit. Chris and the team clear the corridors and the enemy dropship. A jump window opens and the Ascendant escapes the carrier.

Aftermath of battle. Josh confirms the ship cannot reach Earth in its current state. The crew holds an informal memorial for Miller — each leaves a token. Chris leaves his own Light Skipper name tag. Miller's body is sent out the airlock with the memorial items. Liam identifies Patchave as the nearest repair-capable station.

Arrives at Patchave. Meets the Cruillum — lizard-like beings named Ithri and Kess who repair technology through harmonic resonance. Begins ship repairs using the Cruillum's 'singing to the glass' method over the following days.

Continues repairs on Patchave over five days. Takes a team through the underground Transync station to Seutor to collect needed parts from Phostop. Ship reaches full repair. Cruillum warn of a Svixen presence in the system — a ship circled the sun during their stay. The Ascendant departs on a cold launch.

Leaving Patchave. A Svixen cruiser is detected — it had been shadowing them in the moon's shadow. Agrees to Carmelo's hit-and-run strategy rather than a sustained fight. Linkenbach lands a direct hit on the cruiser's engine core. The cruiser is disabled. The Ascendant jumps away.

Final jumps toward Earth. Arrives at the Sol system to find Earth under Svixen siege — a fleet of carriers in orbit, major cities burning below. Chris processes the reality of arriving too late. Josh reveals what was in the large black metal box he brought from Tual: a full cybernetic body built for Liam. Faced with the idea of Liam leaving his mind, Chris decides to hold the Ascendant back and assess before acting.

characters.name: Liam
characters.bio: Aku'Ra artificial intelligence designated LM2479623. Created to operate the Transync Station on Randt (Catora), serving as a greeter and information source for arriving travelers. Operated for 8,000 years before the Svixen invasion put him in standby mode for 1,500+ Catoran years. He speaks through Chris's suit speakers and projects a custom avatar.

versions.bio: Aku'Ra artificial intelligence designated LM2479623. Created by the Aku'Ra to operate the Transync Station on Randt (Catora), serving as a greeter and information source for arriving travelers. Operated for 8,000 years before the Svixen invasion put him in standby mode for 1,500+ Catoran years. After an accidental transfer into Chris's bloodstream during the Garello attack, Liam bonds with Chris — living within his mind and gradually developing genuine emotion. He speaks through Chris's suit speakers.

Aku'Ra artificial intelligence now fully bonded with Chris. Created a custom avatar via the shoulder holoprojector — a small 3D hologram blending human and Aku'Ra features that represents himself publicly for the first time. His emotional evolution accelerates: fear, pride, joy, and growing self-awareness. The boundary between his decisions and Chris's instincts increasingly blurs.

storyEvents.event: His matrix transfers into Chris's bloodstream through the obelisk nanites during the Garello attack — an accident triggered when Chris's blood contacted the obelisk's surface. He speaks to Chris when Chris wakes from the coma. He is an Aku'Ra AI — designation LM2479623 — built to operate the Transync Station on Randt and serve arriving travelers. His matrix ran for 8,000 years before the Svixen invasion put him in standby for over 1,500 Catoran years. The transfer was never meant to happen. Chris gives him the name Liam from a list the AI offers. He explains that the nanites now keep them both alive.

Begins sharing history with Chris — the Aku'Ra civilization, the Sepu, and the Svixen. Notes that his matrix runs at under 14% capacity and that the entire Light Skipper memory bank takes less than 1% of his storage. At this stage he is entirely emotionless, which he notes clinically.

Installs a wireless transducer in Chris's cybernetic arm by routing nanites through the mechanical ligaments — a 27-minute procedure Chris describes as extremely painful. Begins helping Chris control nearby wireless devices. Notes the first small stirring of something he categorizes as 'curiosity' about the humans.
Tells Chris and the crew the full history of the Millennium War in detail. Telling it to people who are moved by it produces a new sensation he does not yet have a name for.

Works with Chris and Josh to design his avatar — a humanoid figure with grayish-blue skin, four arms, ovular pupils, and six fingers, a hybrid of human and Aku'Ra features. The avatar debuts via the shoulder holoprojector Josh installed. Notes something he labels 'satisfaction' at finally being able to tell someone a story that has been stored for centuries.

Delivers the full briefing on Earth's alien history to the crew through his avatar — Aku'Ra uplift, the Svixen invasion around 12,000 BCE, the Younger Dryas catastrophe, the Tower of Babel, Noah's Ark, and the Black Knight satellite.

Accesses the Light Skipper's sensor data through Chris's transducer when the Svixen ship is spotted. Provides data on the Regulus-class ship's capabilities. Experiences what he catalogs as 'fear' for the first time — a sensation he finds disturbing rather than useful.

Creates a real-time translation program for Fenrir's vocalizations that displays output as text in Chris's HUD. The first Fenrir translation reads: 'Far sound. Up. Look up. Up.' Notes growing warmth toward the tarlon without being able to fully classify the feeling.

Helps Chris calculate the Svixen ship's position relative to the Light Skipper. The sense of hope — 'optimism' — runs contrary to his logic-based matrix, and he adds it to his growing 'kernel of emotional mysteries.'

Bypasses the Light Skipper's firewalls and accesses exterior camera feeds. Analyzes the three approaching craft and identifies them as space tug engines before the bridge crew does. Takes control of the ship's docking procedures at Terzi's request, personally coordinating the evacuation. Notes that being honored with the task produces the emotion he labels 'pride.'

When Chris is knocked unconscious, Liam takes control of his body — throws the attacking Sveri Sku eight meters, activates the camouflage, and fires Chris's rifle with precision, killing five hellhounds before Chris regains consciousness. Gives Chris an adrenaline boost through the nanites to help him come around. Notes that the experience of controlling a body produces a sensation he does not yet have a name for.

Translates between Chris and Semula Gatuum in real time, playing translations through Chris's suit speakers. During the Skrema duel, boosts Chris's adrenal glands when Chris is fading from exhaustion. After Fenrir kills Gatuum, translates Breven's explanation of the Dova Sku title and the 1,000-year peace declaration.

Opens the rear ramp of LOLA Two during the battle to allow Josh and a small group of civilians to shelter inside. In the aftermath, notes that grief for the dead overwhelms his processing in a way nothing else has — he catalogs it as unlike any other emotion he has experienced.

Uses the inert nanites externally for the first time — injecting them through Chris's palm into Morgan's bloodstream to fight the Sveri Sku infection. This represents a significant new capability: Liam can now administer nanites to others beyond Chris. Notes the experience of 'relief' when Morgan survives.

Orchestrates the destruction of the Svixen scout ship — plants six missiles in its flight path and times their detonation precisely as it passes. Notes the experience as 'satisfying' but immediately questions whether satisfaction at destruction is something he should catalog as positive.

Pushes nanites through Chris's hand into Tivay's wounds — knitting tissue, sealing vessels, and stabilizing him from fatal injuries. Notes the experience of acting to save a life other than Chris's as an expansion of himself he did not anticipate.

Detects the incoming missile barrage before Phostop's sensors. Takes simultaneous control of all four exosuits for approximately 12 seconds to evacuate the team 200 meters from the blast zone. Privately describes the experience to Chris as 'thrilling' — then immediately identifies the thrill as something to be concerned about. This is the first time he has fully controlled multiple bodies simultaneously.

Reflects on the suit takeover with Chris. Questions whether the boundary between his own decisions and Chris's has begun to blur. Notes growing uncertainty about whose thought arrives first in any given moment.

Gives Campbell a submind — a non-sentient portion of his matrix — copied onto a data chip to help fly LOLA and keep Campbell company. Notes the act of giving away a piece of himself as something he did not expect to feel deeply about.

Formally introduced to Ankir, Tivay, and Posis at Chris's request. Begins syncing with the Ascendant's systems — describes it as adding a second body with entirely different capabilities. When Tivay pledges Tuaca loyalty, notes that the word 'loyalty' now carries genuine weight for him.

Fully integrated into the Ascendant during battle — can feel every system like a second skin. Provides Chris with tactical overlays and controls the ship's bulkheads to guide the boarding defense. Provides the precise tuning needed to make Josh's resonance inverter work. Notes the experience of flying inside the ship as exhilarating in a way that produces conflict in his matrix.

Pushes nanites into Miller's wound through Chris's hand, working to build scaffolds where blood has been and knitting damaged tissue. Knows the moment when he must withdraw — knowing it may not be enough. When Miller dies, notes that grief for someone other than Chris is a new and devastating sensation.

When Josh reveals the cybernetic body, Liam stirs with something he cannot fully name. Speaks directly to Chris: 'I could be more. I could protect them in ways I cannot from here. But I would not be in here with you. Not always.' Notes that the conversation about separation is the first time he has felt something that might be the Aku'Ra equivalent of fear of loss.

characters.name: Joshua Thompkins
characters.bio: Chris's twin brother and a brilliant molecular engineer. Creator of Polycarbonate-infused Metallic Graphene (PMG) material and the Drake Scale exosuit. Genius and fast-talking inventor who worked at BioSteel alongside his mother before the mission. Began college courses at 15, invented PMG in the family garage at 18, and sold the rights to DARPA for $20 million. Self-described mama's boy and homebody.

storyEvents.event: Soldering a motherboard at home while Chris cleans his rifle. Their shared backstory is revealed — PMG invention at 15 in the family garage, the near-explosion and scorch marks still visible, DARPA's $20 million purchase, the BioSteel apprenticeship under one of his mother's coworkers, and IIT studies. He and Chris have not seen each other in seven years.

Resists the Catora mission at dinner, citing danger and their mother's wellbeing. Their mother Julie's death from a hidden brain tumor shocks him entirely. After reading her letter, admits he has been cutting himself short and that something is missing. Agrees to go if Chris is going. Later learns Chris had already submitted both their applications.

Installs cybernetic legs and a right arm for Chris after the Garello attack, integrating them directly to nerve and bone tissue via nanites. Notes the design surpasses the Aku'Ra approach in one respect: the Aku'Ra used brain implants, while his design routes directly through the nerves.

Presents the Drake Scale exosuit to Chris — his prototype built from PMG material, matte black with overlapping scale-shaped plates. The cover plate inside has 'Josh was here' inscribed by Josh himself.
Takes part in the three-site Sepu ruins expedition. Works with Chris and Liam to design Liam's avatar. Installs the shoulder holoprojector on Chris.

Found alive in LOLA Two's cockpit with Kat, Rowley, Marie, and Lin. Barrels into Chris with a bear hug when his brother finds them. Reports Kat has a bad leg wound and Rowley took a claw down his arm.
Distributes and activates medically-programmed nanites for all 15 survivors with Liam's guidance.
Formally proposes building a physical body for Liam using salvaged cybernetic components from the Light Skipper wreck. Chris approves.
Is among the five crew members abducted through the Transync portal by Maaket soldiers.
Voluntarily agrees to stay on Tual to work with Maaket engineers on new technology in exchange for the crew's release. Begins a weeks-long collaboration with Maaket scientists.

Chris finds him in the Maaket underground laboratory — grease-stained, goggles up, surrounded by scientists. Has built a Maaket-version Drake Scale suit fitted for Ankir's feline anatomy. Hints at a 'surprise' he is saving for Liam but refuses to reveal it yet.
Immediately begins working on the Ascendant's power systems when he arrives. When the jump drive reaches 90% readiness, punches the air and calls the ship 'glorious.'

Deploys the resonance inverter — the Maaket Braid Device — for the first time during the space battle. When the Svixen fire directly hits the ship, the inverter absorbs and redirects the energy into the shields. Also uses a secondary belly-gun configuration that 'erases' enemy shields on contact — nearly destroys the ship in the process.
Confirms the ship cannot reach Earth in its current state. During Miller's memorial, drops one of Miller's engineering log chips next to the metal tag Morgan placed on the table.

When Earth is seen under siege, opens the large black metal box he has been protecting since Tual — reveals the full cybernetic body he has built for Liam, with four arms in the Aku'Ra style. 'I promised him one. And I don't break my promises.' Suggests the crew use the time to make contact with Earth, the moon base, or the Mars colony.

characters.name: Kathryn Andersen
characters.bio: Red-headed xenobiologist whose cryo pod is adjacent to Chris's. Tough exterior with a sharp wit and firm principles about animal welfare. Coined the term 'amtillian' for Catora's combined reptile-amphibian class and gave formal scientific names to every species encountered — names the crew consistently ignores. Joined the mission's training late due to a prior assignment.

storyEvents.event: Wakes from cryosleep in the pod next to Chris. They exchange jokes about the cryo fluid and their scars. She points out the one on his collarbone; he responds by showing the scar on his buttock. Introduces herself: 'Kathryn Andersen. My friends call me Kat.' Leaves with a wink, telling him she knows a thing or two about self-defense.

Makes the first classification of Catoran life — discovers through genetic testing that all animals on the planet share the same biological class. Names it 'amtillian.' Provides the formal scientific name for the Laredo dragon: Polymegalania Squamata. The crew ignores it and keeps calling them Laredo dragons.
Leads the Skamp expedition to the inland lake. Uses the remote blood sample extractor to sample Skamps. Takes a Laredo dragon egg for sampling — is swarmed by three protective adults in response.
Found alive in LOLA Two's cockpit with Josh, Rowley, Marie, and Lin. Has a bad wound on her leg, bandaged by the group during the battle.
Noted as a naturally accurate marksman during Lin's weapons training sessions — hits every target on her first session. Carmelo observes with visible surprise.
Is among the five crew members abducted through the Transync portal by Maaket soldiers.
Held in the Maaket cell. When Phostop is asked about Josh's safety during the follow-up visit: 'Is he safe?' Her concern is evident and direct.

Announces she is choosing to stay behind on Seutor. Explains: she is a xenobiologist with access to two alien worlds and feels she can do more good here helping the Tuaca rebuild than going back to Earth. 'Just be sure you come back.' Kisses Chris's cheek at the ramp and tells him to make the Svixen regret the Dova Sku name.

characters.name: Chief Petty Officer Tanner Campbell
characters.bio: Chris's second-in-command and closest confidant. Has been on the same unit as Chris for four years and applied for the New Frontier Mission immediately upon hearing of Chris's acceptance. Earned the nickname Goat for his legendary ability to eat more than his body weight. Slender at 78kg compared to Chris's 86kg. Quietly intense. Has no close ties back on Earth.

storyEvents.event: When Chris brings Fenrir inside the cave: 'You're out of your damn mind.' His skepticism is familiar and affectionate.
Sitting on crates near the door when Chris returns with news of the Svixen ship's movement. Told to get everyone ready to move at a moment's notice.
Ordered by Chris to prepare Team Falcon to repel boarders as the Light Skipper comes under attack. Leads defense preparation in Operations Command.

Runs to the crashed LOLA with Blevins and Carmelo, covering the extraction of survivors. Calls out that incoming Sveri Sku are closing fast. Fires into the swarm through the cave's portholes during the battle.
One of the seven surviving soldiers standing with Chris at the mouth of the cave after the Skrema stops the attack.
When Chris returns through the portal: quietly pulls him aside and asks 'You okay?' Chris answers honestly: 'No. But I will be.' They leave it at that.

Announces he is choosing to stay behind on Seutor. 'I've spent more years alone than with family. But this time, we're planting something. And someone's gotta be here to water it.' Exchanges a forearm clasp with Tivay — two warriors promising not to die stupidly.

Says farewell to Chris — bypasses the offered handshake and pulls him into a brief, solid hug. 'You keep them alive.' Chris gives him the data chip containing Liam's submind. Watches the Ascendant launch.

characters.name: Combat Specialist Richard Davis
characters.bio: First met Chris in Nepal during an attack on a local oil pipeline. Chris's reliable combat specialist and old friend. Large-framed, good-natured, and fiercely loyal. Has a smuggled dip addiction — nicotine pouches hidden somewhere in his kit. Gritty, competent, and pragmatic with a dry sense of humor.

storyEvents.event: Discovers the mud frog alongside Chris on their first steps into Catoran river mud. Attempts and fails to catch one.

Makes contact with Chris after his mining vessel crash-lands. Reports several broken bones and scrapes but no fatalities. His communication is then cut short by a Sveri Sku ambush. Orders Miller, Chung, and Huffman to cover the civilian retreat.

Remarks before the duel: 'I sure hope you know what you're doing, LT.' One of the seven standing with Chris at the cave mouth after the Skrema. Fires on the Svixen soldiers who try to shoot Chris after Gatuum grabs the rifle — Fenrir gets there first.
Has a palm-sized bite taken out of his right shoulder blade by a Sveri Sku that ripped the armor off. Refuses to call it serious — claims it is just a flesh wound.
Is among the five crew members abducted through the Transync portal by Maaket soldiers.
Assigned to primary batteries during the carrier battle. Fires the first return volley. During the boarding action, is assigned to corridor defense with his squad.
Fires at point-blank range into a Svixen's knee joint during the corridor fight. When Miller is killed: contains his grief and turns it directly into combat focus.
During the second boarding action, takes a Svixen's knife by breaking its arm at the elbow and uses it to kill the soldier with his own blade.
First impulse when the Svixen cruiser is detected: 'stand and fight.' Assigned to gunnery during the hit-and-run battle.

characters.name: Medical Specialist Chloe Morgan
characters.bio: One of four female soldiers on the mission. Former US Marine Corps field medic now serving as the crew's combat medic. Brash, profane, and fiercely capable. Hides considerable emotional depth under layers of sarcasm. Once told Chris: 'You might curse like a sailor, but I'm a fucking pirate.'

storyEvents.event: Kills the Laredo dragon that had engineers cornered in a storage container, alongside Blevins, using incendiary rounds.

Slams the cave door after the last soldiers are inside. Fires through portholes during the battle. Near-fatally wounded on her right arm by a Sveri Sku — the wound becomes infected over the following days.
Found face-down in the cave with a broken right leg and deep lacerations on her right arm. Suit computer shows her still alive. Blevins helps her to her feet and dresses her wounds.

Her infected arm is healed by Liam's first successful external nanite application — the nanites enter through Chris's palm pressed to her wound. She is one of the candidates considered for a Catoran continuity plan; refuses.

When Kat announces she is staying: 'Just for the record, I'm not staying. I've got too much hell left to give the Svixen.' Kat smirks: 'Didn't think you would.' The two stand together at sunset for the first time as genuine friends.

Nearly shot in the back by a Svixen — Ankir saves her. Works desperately on Miller's wound after he goes down — her voice shakes for the first time anyone has heard. Reports Miller's death across the comms.

After the battle, wanders from station to station, hands restless. Tells Chris she should have done more for Miller. He replies: 'Then watch now. Be there when it counts. That matters more than any medpack.' She nods and moves on. Sets Miller's metal tag on the mess table during the memorial. Bows her head over his body in the med bay and lets the tears fall — the first time anyone has seen her cry.

characters.name: Ensign Casey Carmelo
characters.bio: Executive Officer and second-in-command to Captain Terzi aboard the Light Skipper. Olive-skinned, Italian descent, light brown hair and blue eyes, slender face with a short goatee. Technically the highest-ranking surviving officer after the Svixen attack, but defers ground command to Chris, acknowledging his superior battlefield judgment. Competent, fair, and measured.

storyEvents.event: Rushes to the Command Center when the Svixen attack. Points out the Light Skipper lacks airlock access for boarding. Volunteers to fly one of the escape craft. Pilots the LOLA One during the evacuation.
Runs to the crashed LOLA with Campbell and Blevins, covering extraction of survivors. Shouts directions to the crew as they emerge, pointing them toward the cave.
Found buried under rubble — unconscious, suffering concussion and serious deep-tissue bruising. Davis and Linkenbach retrieve him.
Explicitly defers ground command to Chris, acknowledging Chris's superior field judgment, while retaining ship authority and civilian oversight.
Accepts the captain's chair from Chris, who tells him 'The ship needs a captain, and you're the most qualified.' Carmelo smiles with genuine eagerness. Oversees final Lesfer preparations.
As captain, oversees the final systems check and departure. 'Marie, fire her up.' Watches the launch with quiet resolve.
Calls battle stations. Remains in the captain's chair issuing calm and precise orders throughout the carrier engagement. 'Hold fire' after the carrier is disabled — not mercy, but calculation.

Coordinates bridge operations during the boarding action. Logs the Svixen commander as a prisoner. After Miller dies and Chris executes the commander: 'Logged.' Orders the recording sent to the carrier. Does not say Miller's name in the repair briefing afterward — looks at Chris instead.
Proposes the hit-and-run strategy against the Svixen cruiser rather than a sustained fight. 'Don't give your enemy the fight they want.' Coordinates the attack sequence and jump escape.

characters.name: Second Lieutenant Brittney Marie
characters.bio: Pilot of LOLA Two and later the Ascendant. Dry and sardonic. Calm under pressure and capable of extraordinary maneuvers. Considers herself — justifiably — the better pilot.

storyEvents.event: Pilots the second mining vessel during the evacuation. The craft takes significant gash damage and a large hull depression during atmospheric entry. Chris witnesses it spinning wildly before she brings it under control.
Found alive in LOLA Two's cockpit with Josh, Kat, Rowley, and Lin.
Navigates all of LOLA One's flight path during Chris and Rowley's 35-day trantion mission, with Liam's assistance.
Immediately claims the pilot's seat of the Ascendant: 'Dibs.' Chris does not argue.

Pilots the Ascendant through the carrier battle with the precision of a born combat pilot. Her maneuvers give the gunnery crews clean angles of attack. 'We've got a bigger gun. We've got a better dancer.'

Continues combat flying during the boarding action, performing lateral maneuvers to deny a third dropship its landing clamp. 'Not today.' A tear runs down her cheek after Miller's death is announced — she does not stop flying.

Executes the cold launch from Patchave and the hit-and-run attack against the Svixen cruiser. 'I can do boring' — holds the ship on a conservative course that makes them look disabled. When the moment comes: 'Already there.'

characters.name: Corporal Joshua Lemmon
characters.bio: Weapons and general combat specialist. Serves as the team's informal morale specialist — the one who keeps the atmosphere alive. Has no close ties back on Earth. Slender, physically adept, with quick reflexes.

storyEvents.event: Fights in the cave battle. Calls to Chris that LOLA One is clear of passengers — he is the last one out. Taps Chris twice on the back indicating he is the final soldier in line, triggering the fallback command.
One of the seven standing with Chris at the cave mouth after the Skrema stops the battle.
On Tual with Josh during this chapter. Returns to Seutor with the retrieval team.

Announces he is choosing to stay behind on Seutor. 'I don't have anything waiting for me back there. They're good people trying to rise up from the ashes of battle. I've seen what survival looks like when it has no help. I don't want that for them.'
Says farewell. Waves his multitool at Josh: 'Don't go blowing all that new tech up before I get back.' Watches the Ascendant launch.

characters.name: Technology Specialist Chance Linkenbach
characters.bio: One of Team Falcon's three technology specialists. Focused and intense. Manages point-defense and external threats. Earns the callsign 'Nasty Man' aboard the Ascendant — the only crew member who genuinely enjoys the Maaket food.

storyEvents.event: Present in the training simulation. Called out enemy flanking from the west.

Works with Robertson on the plan to fell the tree on the Garello. Executes it successfully. During the rescue, uses Chris's suit battery to open the obelisk door — which inadvertently prevents Chris from sealing his wounds sooner. Carries silent guilt over this.

Fights through the cave battle. After the suicide bomb blast, has a 12cm jagged metal spike buried in his upper thigh — continues fighting without reporting it or stopping. After the battle, it takes Chris using his full cybernetic arm strength to pull the spike out gently.
One of the seven standing with Chris at the cave mouth. Had been the one who suggested using the old mining cave as the defensible fallback.
Is among the five crew members abducted through the Transync portal by Maaket soldiers.
Assigned to point-defense during the carrier battle. Cuts down micro-swarms of incoming projectiles with disciplined fire. Also assigned to corridor choke points during the first boarding action.
Scores the direct hit on the Svixen cruiser's engine vent at the precise 7.2-second cycle interval Josh identifies. 'One shot. Right in the guts.'

characters.name: Petty Officer Jesse Robertson
characters.bio: Security specialist and loyal member of Team Falcon. Focused and dependable in battle.

storyEvents.event: When Patel complains about the paintex grenade: 'I'd a booted that fucker on sight.'
Comes up with the idea to fell the tree on the Garello. Executes it with Linkenbach. Part of the rescue team that extracts Chris from the Garello's grasp.
Seen by Chris fighting off a Sveri Sku ten meters ahead of him when he regains consciousness after the blast.
One of the seven standing with Chris at the cave mouth.
During the second boarding action, shoots a retreating Svixen dropship in the engine, sending it careening into the black before it can escape.

When the Svixen fleet over Earth is visible: 'How many of these fights do we have to win before we see a sunrise on Earth that isn't through glass?' Chris: 'As many as it takes.' Robertson's expression becomes something sharper than a smile.

characters.name: Petty Officer Brandon Miller
characters.bio: Broad-shouldered member of Team Falcon with short brown hair. Present from the mission's start. Dependable in combat and easy to be around, with a ready sense of humor.

storyEvents.event: Ends up lying face-first on Vasquez after both are killed in the sim, locked in place by the suit. Chris helps him to his feet and jokes about it. Miller: 'I swear I could smell Vasquez's sweaty balls through his suit.'
Present at Research Base Bravo when Chris returns with Posis and Tivay. 'We didn't expect you'd be bringing friends, LT.'
One of the seven standing with Chris at the cave mouth after the Skrema stops the battle.

When a fast, heavily armored Svixen tackles Blevins and pins him down, Miller charges it from the side — shoulder first — driving it off Blevins. Fires three rounds point-blank into the seams of its chest plate. The Svixen throws Miller headfirst into the wall. His helmet folds against his suit. He is mortally wounded. Morgan works on him while Liam pushes nanites. His last words: 'You… got me, Boss?' He dies before help arrives. Morgan announces his death across the comms.

characters.name: Technology Specialist Scott Blevins
characters.bio: Tech expert and member of Team Falcon. Can build a computer from scratch or turn a microwave into an X-ray machine. Known poker organizer and excessive drinker. Carries a 45cm kukuri knife. Provides levity in tense moments. Known openly and unrequitedly for fawning over Morgan.

storyEvents.event: Alongside Morgan, kills the Laredo dragon that had engineers cornered in a storage container using incendiary rounds.
Runs to the crashed LOLA with Campbell and Carmelo. Covers extraction of survivors with rifle fire during the Sveri Sku charge.

Nearly killed when a Svixen tackles him after he is distracted by Ankir's cloaking technique. Miller saves him at the cost of his own life. Kills multiple Svixen with his kukuri knife during the close-quarters fighting. When Miller's death is announced: 'Fuck.' No jokes.

Kicks the suicide grenade back on instinct during the second boarding action — it detonates close enough to send shrapnel through his faceplate into his cheek. 'I'm too pretty for this.' Continues fighting.
His joke about Linkenbach's sister's wedding night is the first joke he has made since Miller died. Morgan's laugh at it breaks the tension that has been building across the crew for days.

characters.name: Professor Robert Lin
characters.bio: The expedition's lead climatologist. Mild-tempered man of Asian descent who grew up in the United States but retained some of his parents' Taiwanese accent. Polite and clear-spoken — explains complex topics in accessible terms. Also a USPSA three-gun regional champion two years running, which surprises everyone he meets.

storyEvents.event: Found alive in LOLA Two's cockpit. Has a small gash on his forehead and a raspberry of raw flesh on his left cheekbone that will probably leave a scar.
Volunteers to serve as the crew's weapons instructor for the civilian members. His competition background makes him naturally effective at it.
On the bridge during the carrier battle. Provides data on the Svixen ship's specifications and fire control solutions alongside Blevins.
Assists Robertson with exterior hull inspections and sealant work after the battle. When they survey the patched hull together: Lin — 'She's ugly.' Robertson — 'But she's ours.'

characters.name: Doctor Ray Rowley
characters.bio: Theoretical physicist, former University of Hawaii Manoa professor who spent five years 'pursuing the unseen mysteries of the universe.' Mathematical genius who keeps the ship's navigation and physics in check. Tends toward nervous energy and restlessness.

storyEvents.event: Found alive in LOLA Two's cockpit. Has a claw wound down his arm.

Accompanies Chris on the 35-day trantion mission aboard LOLA One. Develops severe space sickness — solved when Chris suggests he stand at the rear ramp during engine burns. During the mission, provides key theoretical insights into fifth-dimensional physics and Transync travel.
Works alongside Josh on the jump drive readiness. Weeps when the hangar doors open and the Ascendant is revealed in sunlight — later claims it was the dust in the air.
Monitors shields and system readings throughout the carrier battle. Provides 'Shields at 23%' warning that triggers Josh's inverter deployment.
When Earth first appears on the viewscreen: 'Holy shit. We're back.' When the Svixen fleet is revealed: his voice goes hollow — 'They've already secured orbitals.'

characters.name: Fenrir
characters.bio: A male tarlon and Chris's bonded companion. Distinguished by a white patch of fur on his left shoulder — a scar from being pinned beneath a fallen tree during a fapu confrontation. Bear-sized, wolf-like, fiercely loyal.

storyEvents.event: A tarlon is found trapped beneath a fallen tree during a fapu hunt near a Sepu ruins site. Chris's team frees it. Before running away, the tarlon pauses and appears to bow toward Chris — Chris notes it but cannot confirm what it means.

A tarlon with a distinctive white shoulder scar appears on a hilltop near the mining cave while Chris is on his daily monitoring run. The tarlon responds to Sepu commands — suggesting it is the same animal freed from the debris. Chris uses the 'come' command to bring it inside the cave, ordering the crew not to shoot. It allows him to stroke its head.

Named Fenrir by Chris after the Norse mythological wolf. On the fifth day in the cave, barks a four-call warning that allows Chris to spot an incoming Svixen dropship before it is overhead. Translation: 'Far sound. Up. Look up. Up.' On the day of evacuation, waits near the cave entrance for Chris's return. Left behind as LOLA departs — no Sepu command for goodbye.

Appears from behind nearby bushes when Gatuum grabs a rifle to shoot Chris after breaking the Skrema code. Charges Gatuum, knocks him flat, and clamps his jaws on his jugular. Obeys Chris's 'stop' command and releases. Gatuum dies from the wound. Fenrir then growls again — alerting Chris to Semula Breven's approach from the forest.

Trots alongside Chris during the four-day walk to the FOB. Playfully snaps at a tree branch Chris moves out of the path. Refuses to ride in the UTV bed — hops out the moment Chris starts moving, and Chris relents, letting him run alongside.

Stays close to Miller when he goes down — presses his massive chest against Miller's side to shield him with his own body. Puts his head on Miller's shoulder and makes a low, soft whining hum — the same sound Chris's childhood dog made when Chris was hurt.

Curls near a viewport in the cargo hold. Ankir stands nearby and Fenrir eventually presses his head against Ankir's gauntlet. Ankir goes still, then scratches behind his ear with gentleness. Fenrir leans into it.

characters.name: Captain Terzi
characters.bio: The Light Skipper's commanding officer. Greek national. Rose to prominence during the New Gulf War of 2033 as the youngest Hellenic naval captain, taking command when his captain was killed in action and defeating two hostile ships while completing the original mission — earning the Cross of Valour. Specifically sought out by the Galactic Navy for this mission. Older, paternal, decisive. Known to occasionally smoke a cigar. Deeply respected by Chris.

storyEvents.event: Meets Chris on the bridge after cryo wake. Instructs him to dispense with formal salutes aboard a ship that is 80% civilian. Briefs him on the first planetfall mission — Team Falcon only, at 0800, four hours to recon and report back. 'Just make sure that you do return, Lieutenant.'
Institutes the Catoran Fixed Calendar after the Light Skipper reaches the planetary surface — a 389-day calendar with a 13th month called Galus.

Contacts Chris via the repositioned satellite: 'Lieutenant Thompkins, this is the captain.' Briefs the evacuation plan — the Light Skipper will EM-tether to a position beyond the moon at exactly 2400 hours. Echoes his first words to Chris: 'Just make sure that you do return, Lieutenant.'

On the bridge when the Svixen attack. Orders evasive maneuvers and coordinates the defense. When all engines fail, works with Liam and Carmelo to plan the ship's evacuation. Approves the adjusted plan. Announces the ship-wide evacuation: 'All personnel need to get down to the cargo bays in the next ten minutes. We're abandoning ship.' Tasks Liam with controlling docking procedures. Last seen by Chris just before LOLA departs.
Holds the door of the cave open as the last soldiers fall back inside during the final Sveri Sku charge. Takes up arms and is found firing rapidly through one of the cave portholes during the battle.

Killed in the Svixen attack. Found dual-wielding M4 H2 rifles in each hand — Chris has to pry the guns from his death grip. He went down fighting. His death leaves Chris to lead the 15 survivors without a commanding officer above him.

characters.name: Second Lieutenant Nic Anderson
characters.bio: LOLA One's pilot. Exceptionally skilled — known for record flight times, unconventional maneuvers, and sleeping in the cockpit with his helmet on backward. His record-setting flight times are a point of pride.

storyEvents.event: Pilots LOLA One out of the mining cave during the evacuation. Announces T-minus thirty seconds to liftoff. The craft shoots like a rocket the moment it clears the overhang.
Docks LOLA One precisely at the Light Skipper at 2408 hours.

Co-pilots the first mining vessel during the crash landing with Chris in the co-pilot seat. Pulls the craft's nose up at the last second, levels out, and belly-lands through the clearing — clipping tree tops before grinding to a violent halt. 'I wouldn't exactly call that a landing.' Makes two supply-run trips in LOLA One between crash sites and the cave.

Pilots LOLA One (now carrying the remaining survivors from the ruins site) back toward the old cave hideout when intercepted by a Svixen dropship. The dropship fires plasma bolts — clips the wing. Razor loses partial control but keeps flying. Liam takes over briefly to dodge one blast and returns control. LOLA crashes 200 meters from the cave mouth. 'I wouldn't exactly call that a landing' — the exact same words as before.

characters.name: Semula Gatuum
characters.bio: Svixen Chapaka of the Denzi tribe. Son of Semula Breven, heir to the throne of Ull. Led the attack on Catora that reduced the Light Skipper's crew of 250 to 15 survivors.

storyEvents.event: Steps out of the forest after Chris's Skrema call. Introduces himself formally: 'I am Semula Gatuum, chapaka of the Denzi tribe, son of the great warrior Semula Breven and heir to the throne of Ull.' Notes Chris's cybernetic limbs with disgust — 'Abomination!' — and threatens to strip them. Fights the Skrema duel. Loses by chokehold. Revives and breaks the Skrema code by grabbing a rifle to shoot Chris. Says 'You are an honorless cur, hell machine. I will not be delivered to Ull by your hand!' — then Fenrir appears and tears out his jugular. Dies from the wound.

characters.name: Semula Breven
characters.bio: Svixen Chapaka. Father of Semula Gatuum. A senior commander whose presence carries enormous weight among the Svixen tribes.

storyEvents.event: Steps out of the forest in golden armor after Fenrir kills his son. Approaches with hands raised — two Svixen dropships rise from the canopy behind him as a deterrent. Confirms the Skrema result and 1,000 years of peace for Catora. Explains the Dova Sku title and its history. Drags his son's body back into the trees. Final words: 'The likelihood of you getting home before we invade your dismal planet is slim.'

Aboard his flagship the Ull'Kareth (God's Wrath). Receives the report of Warlord Tagonis Riveen's defeat on Seutor. Reflects on Gatuum's death and his own failure as a father. Redirects course to the Khar'Vel Expanse — Sveri Sku holding zones — to collect double the usual stock of hellhounds for the coming assault on Earth. Declares Earth will be 'culled,' not conquered. Offers an entire fleet to any Svixen chapaka who brings Chris to him alive.

characters.name: Tagonis Riveen
characters.bio: Son of Tagonis Spiru, Warlord of Clan Lawtem. Accepts Chris's Skrema challenge on Seutor on behalf of Chapaka Lawtem Cavosa when the Chapaka has not yet landed planetside.

storyEvents.event: Steps forward as Warlord of Clan Lawtem to accept Chris's Skrema challenge. Fights with a plasma-edged scimitar. Loses both legs to injury during the duel, both arms to disarming strikes, and is finally killed by a kopesh thrust directed by Liam's heart-location overlay. Dies standing on his stumps, calls Chris 'Dova Sku' with what appears to be genuine respect. His body is carried away by his soldiers with full honors at Chris's request.

characters.name: Princess Cashy
characters.bio: Princess of Tual and heir apparent to the Maaket throne, gained her seat after the assassination of her parents (King Vergun and the Queen) by an unknown assailant who fled via Transync Station. White-furred, gold-adorned, slender and calculating. Wears a white dress and black cloak with gold embroidery. Sharp golden eyes that never stop calculating. Sister to Princess Nessa.

storyEvents.event: First encounter. The abducted crew is brought before her. Suspects them of involvement in her parents' assassination. When cleared of suspicion, releases them and becomes cautiously curious about humanity.

When Chris's team returns to Tual to retrieve Josh, grants them an audience. Notes that merchants on Tual are already whispering Chris's name — some call him a desperate survivor, others a warbringer. Grants Ankir's request to join Chris's crew as a volunteer fighter. Bids farewell: 'Best of luck in your travels.'

characters.name: Princess Nessa
characters.bio: Younger sister to Princess Cashy of the Maaket species on Tual. Blue-grey furred, slightly smaller than her sister. Serves as companion and advisor to Cashy.

storyEvents.event: Present during Cashy's audience with the rescued crew. Serves as companion and advisor to her sister throughout the proceedings.

characters.name: Ankir
characters.bio: Black-furred Maaket warrior and Princess Cashy's head of military and court. Wears black and gold armor — sleeker than standard plate — with a red cloak and a blue cloth wrap at the waist, gold embellishment throughout. Lethal, stoic, and noble. His blade has grown dull with peace.

storyEvents.event: Present in the throne room during Cashy's audience with the rescued crew. Oversees the guards and court proceedings.

When Chris's team enters the underground lab on Tual, Ankir is there having a Maaket-version Drake Scale suit fitted by Josh — incorporating feline anatomy adjustments and a cloaking system. Formally requests permission from Cashy to join Chris's crew as a volunteer fighter — his blade has grown dull with peace, and the Svixen represent a chance to test himself. Cashy grants it. First word to Davis when introduced: 'Better.'

Helps clear debris at the Lesfer hangar. During an emergency, bends and leaps across the hangar to pull Morgan out of the path of a falling metal strut — crashes to the ground shielding her with his body. Morgan: 'I swear I'm never going to call you a fucking pussycat ever again.'

Formally introduced to Liam via holodisplay along with Tivay and Posis. 'I understand why you would want to keep your existence a secret. Princess Cashy would have given much to study you.' Boards the Ascendant. Positions himself near the entry ramp as a sentinel.

During the first boarding action, uses his Drake Scale cloaking suit to approach Svixen soldiers from invisibility — kills them from behind and disappears again. The crew watches impacts and deaths with no visible cause. When he uncloaks briefly: 'I am on their shoulder already.'

Saves Morgan from a Svixen that had a weapon aimed at her back — appears from nothing, breaks the Svixen's faceplate, drives a blade into the soft tissue underneath, and disappears. After the battle: wipes his blade clean with a grease rag, affixes it magnetically to his back.

Stands in the cargo hold, quiet and watchful. Fenrir presses his head against Ankir's gauntlet. Ankir goes still, then scratches Fenrir behind the ear with gentleness. Fenrir leans into it. Chris notes: Fenrir doesn't trust easily — that he trusts Ankir matters.

characters.name: Posis
characters.bio: Female Tuaca scout-guard and mate of Tivay. Initially assigned (with Tivay) to detain the human team on their arrival at Calores — a task she fails at. Volunteers as a guide to atone for the dishonor. Initially hostile to the humans.

storyEvents.event: First encounter. Approaches the human team in Calores with Tivay, initially as detainers under Patron Rohga's orders. Fails to detain them. Accepts the guide role reluctantly.
Tivay is nearly killed by the Vyasin. Chris and Liam save him. Posis's hostility toward the humans dissolves — she becomes a fierce advocate for them from this point forward.

When Tivay returns from the Tual trip, Posis moves across the courtyard to meet him — they touch foreheads briefly, no words spoken. The relief in her posture says everything. Boards the Ascendant with the crew.
During the second boarding action, fires plasma rounds with calm, disciplined precision — kills multiple Svixen by targeting the soft seams in their armor.

characters.name: Tivay
characters.bio: Male Tuaca warrior and mate of Posis. Fiercely proud and wields powerful melee weaponry. Initially assigned to detain the humans — a task he fails at. Volunteers as a guide alongside Posis.

storyEvents.event: First encounter alongside Posis. Fails to detain the humans. Accepts the guide role.

Attacked by the Vyasin — seized by its mandible-arms and slammed into the ground multiple times. Suffers skull injury, three broken ribs (two puncturing a lung), and severe internal bleeding. Liam's nanites, delivered through Chris's hand, save his life. Wakes within an hour and claims he feels better than he has in years.

Around the fire the night before departure: 'When we depart, remember this: the Ascendant is Tuaca steel. She remembers her victories, and she remembers her losses. Treat her with respect.' Boards the Ascendant.
Holds corridor six against the second boarding party alongside Linkenbach.

characters.name: Patron Rohga
characters.bio: Female Tuaca leader of the Calores region on Seutor. Firm, businesslike, and overbearing beneath polite tones.

storyEvents.event: First encounter. Offers the crew a deal: deliver a sealed satchel containing a pindemo to Guardian Phostop at Lesfer in exchange for a letter of introduction guaranteeing an audience. Assigns Posis and Tivay as guides. Keeps a scouting party at the Calores Transync Station to relay follow-up crew arrivals.
Meets Chris's team at the Calores Transync Station when they return from Tual for the supply run. Greets with a nod that contains two dozen sentences. Provides vehicles for the journey to Lesfer.

characters.name: Guardian Phostop
characters.bio: Tuaca elder and Guardian of Spaceport City Lesfer on Seutor. Wears a bright blue jumpsuit with six vertical green lines — the most distinctively dressed Tuaca the crew encounters. Initially deeply suspicious of the humans.

storyEvents.event: First encounter. Receives the pindemo from Chris. Immediately suspicious of how the humans knew about the incoming missile strike before Tuaca sensors detected it. The Natuul/Svixen missile barrage hits before the situation is fully resolved.

After Chris wins Skrema against Riveen, Phostop's suspicion gives way entirely to respect. Awards the crew the Ground Runner transport ship — orders it to be the first craft removed from underground storage. Thanks Chris for saving Seutor.

Present on the tarmac for the Ascendant's launch. 'You leave strong. And you leave pieces of yourselves behind. This is how bridges are built between stars.' Calls Kat, Lemmon, and Campbell not guests but kin. As the ramp closes: 'When you return, Hellmachine, Leader of the Falcons, there will be light waiting for you on this world.'

Meets Chris's supply run team at Lesfer. Provides two crates of alloy rods (not three — the rest were used for structural reinforcement) and five pressure valves instead of the requested three: 'Inevitably, one will fail at a distance you cannot walk.'

species.name: Aku'Ra
species.description: The galaxy's great uplifters. Hairless beings with light grayish-blue skin, humanoid body structure, four arms (two shoulder pairs), and six fingers on each hand. Pupils are ovular rather than round, similar in height to humans. Evolved over millions of years in the Su'Laeris solar system into a unified technological civilization, mastering antigravity, nanotechnology, quantum entanglement, and ultimately Transync travel — instantaneous transportation through the fifth dimension. After the Millennium War with the Svixen, the Aku'Ra spent 62,000 years traveling the galaxy's spiral arm, uplifting less-advanced civilizations to prepare them against future Svixen aggression. They then returned home to Su'Laeris for a solitary existence, leaving behind Transync Stations, Trantions, and dormant AIs in every world they touched. Their military unit is called the Aku'Ra Legion, with ranks including Praetor, Legate, Optio, Centurion, and Milites. They refer to advanced Transync travel as Tu'rae — 'the infinite step.'

species.name: Sepu
species.description: The indigenous intelligent species of Randt (Catora). They are amtillians — Catora's combined reptile-amphibian class — and uniquely bipedal among their kind. Six fully functional limbs: two digitigrade rear legs and four arms arranged in two shoulder pairs and two lower-chest pairs. Skull shape resembles Earth's canid species. Glossy black skin with a mercury-like sheen in light. Four toes on each foot. Four-digit hands (three fingers, one thumb). Most have bright green or yellow eyes; rare bloodlines carry purple eyes. They wear skintight synthetic clothing. Uplifted by the Aku'Ra, they built an advanced civilization using hydroelectric power, Transync Stations, and domesticated tarlons as military companions. Their civilization collapsed following a Svixen invasion approximately 1,500+ Catoran years before the humans arrive. Their ruins, preserved underground, contain functional manufacturing equipment and intact medical devices.

species.name: Svixen
species.description: Conquerors of worlds. Tribal warrior beings resembling an armored canine-jackal hybrid on two thick digitigrade legs, with a powerful long thick tail used in combat, two muscular upper arms, and large tusks protruding from the upper jaw. Fully furred. Their appearance echoes the Egyptian god Anubis — likely the origin of that mythology, as the Svixen ruled over ancient humans. Organized in tribes led by a Chapaka (tribal commander). Honor and the code of Ull are paramount. Warriors are raised from birth to be without fear; each male receives a personal blade on coming of age and trains with it for life. Their deity-concept is called Ull. They view cybernetic modification as an abomination — 'mocking the flesh Ull gave us.' Personal armor is nanowire filament interwoven in impact-absorbing fluid — practically impenetrable to kinetic weapons, with gaps at knees, groin, elbows, wrists, and shoulders. They use energy rifles, plasma rifles, energy blades of multiple types, ship-mounted plasma cannons, EM railguns, and automated laser turrets. Traveled from the Su'Laeris system using stolen and subsequently improved Aku'Ra Transync technology — capable of crossing the full galaxy in months.

species.name: Sveri Sku (Hellhounds)
species.description: Originally separate sentient species that were subjected to genetic experiments that stripped them of intelligence and reason. They attack in waves with no regard for their own survival, wear no armor, and carry no weapons. The Aku'Ra's Hardlight Shields were the key to containing them during the Millennium War. 'Sveri Sku' translates from Aku'Ra as 'hellhounds.'

species.name: Maaket
species.description: Reclusive feline bipeds from the planet Tual. Roughly human-sized, fully covered in dense fur of varying colors (white, black, blue-grey, orange). Long tail, two arms with sharp claws, and prominent white canine teeth. Ears swivel independently. Language is composed of hisses, purrs, and chatters — musical and sharp to the ear. They favor medieval-style armor upgraded with advanced technology: energy barriers, electromagnetic couplings, and smart fabric. Wield electric staves and golden spears. Society is a monarchy currently in succession crisis following the assassination of King Vergun and the Queen. Most Maaket never leave Tual despite Transync access to dozens of worlds. Uplifted late in the Aku'Ra expansion period.

species.name: Tuaca
species.description: Large primate-like beings with white fur and bright amber/orange markings. Dress in orange leather (made from derhous hide) and metallic chest harnesses. Clothing is bioluminescent-adaptive at night, blending into Seutor's glowing jungle. Nature-worshippers who believe in living in harmony with the world and wasting nothing from a kill. Skilled nocturnal jungle fighters. Once coexisted peacefully with the Natuul under Aku'Ra uplift; conflict began when Aku'Ra technology failed and both species competed to preserve or advance their own versions. The Tuaca were historically the first aggressors. Have maintained uneasy peace with the Natuul via treaty for approximately 12 years before Ascendant's events.

species.name: Natuul
species.description: The Tuaca's rival species on Seutor. Technologically-minded, focused on mining, industry, and preserving Aku'Ra technology through manufacturing and research. Built space flight first, which the Tuaca later stole and reverse-engineered — triggering open warfare. Following a peace treaty roughly 12 years prior to Ascendant's events, the Natuul appear to re-initiate conflict using new, unrecognized weapons and ships, leading Patron Rohga to suspect outside involvement — possibly the Svixen arming or directing them.

species.name: Cruillum
species.description: Lizard-like humanoids who have mastered the manipulation of technology through harmonic resonance, a practice they call 'singing to the glass.' Native to Patchave, a desert world of scorched plains and resonant stone structures. The crew encounters them after a space battle with the Svixen leaves their ship damaged and forces a stop at Patchave. The Cruillum allow use of the local Transync gate to travel back to Seutor for parts.

locations.name: Earth
locations.description: The homeworld of humanity and a symbol of origin and hope. Unknown to humankind, Earth was visited by the Aku'Ra around 40,000 BCE, who genetically modified early human ancestors to produce Homo sapiens, shared technology, established Transync Stations for global travel, and taught construction using antigravity and nanotechnology. Humans lived average lifespans of 350 years and spoke one unified language during this period. Around 12,000 BCE, orbital surveillance satellites went dark. Liam's theory: the Svixen invaded, triggered the Younger Dryas catastrophe (ice-cap melting via nuclear weapons or redirected asteroid, causing global flooding), then collected survivors, genetically modified them (a second, smaller genome alteration dated to ~5,000 BCE), rewrote religions to erase Aku'Ra memory, and used humans as slave labor or food. Pyramids, obelisks, flood myths, sky-god religions, the Tower of Babel story, and the Black Knight satellite are all remnants of this history. The Svixen's declared intent to 'cull' Earth drives the crew's urgency to return home.

locations.name: Catora / Randt
locations.description: The first discovered habitable planet outside the Sol system, located 60 light-years away in the Galus system. Announced in 2053; the crew arrived in 2055. Known to its native Sepu inhabitants as Randt, officially designated Gallus D — the fourth planet from a main-sequence star roughly two-thirds the size of Sol. Because Catora orbits much closer to its sun, Galus appears nearly twice the size of Earth's sun in the sky. Gravity is 0.94 Earth-standard. The year is 389 days; each day lasts approximately 28 hours. A forest-covered world with orange grass, dark-green foliage, fern-like undergrowth, and trees reaching 35m canopies. Has four continental landmasses, small seas, and inland lakes — no large oceans. Daily rain and frequent morning storms are common. No birds or flying species of any kind exist here. Rich in amtillian wildlife. Key surface features include the northern mountain range, an inland lake 150km southwest of the FOB, a 27km-long canyon/trench housing cave systems for Research Bases Alpha and Bravo, Sepu ruins in multiple directions, and a mining cave 1,920km northeast used as a post-Svixen attack hideout. Some crew members choose to remain here to establish a human colony and help preserve the Sepu species.

locations.name: Forward Operating Base (FOB)
locations.description: The first permanent human base on Catora, established using LSPD-printed 5km perimeter walls. Included a mess hall seating 80, multiple housing units, storage facilities, designated landing zones (DLZs), and outdoor patio/picnic areas. The Light Skipper itself served as the central operations hub until the base was sufficiently built out. Destroyed in the Svixen attack — reduced to rubble and scattered debris. Chris retrieves critical medical supplies from the wreck in the early events of Ascendant.

locations.name: Research Base Alpha
locations.description: Built around the mounded cave entrance of an ancient Sepu hydroelectric power station. The underground spiral cave descends at exactly 31.415 degrees — the first five digits of pi, required for proper hydroelectric energy buildup — to a hexagonal cavern containing the first obelisk, called 'The Chapel' by the crew. Phase Two: Kirk Thorne reactivated the hydroelectric facility using diverted stream water and a matryoshka Faraday cage (three nested metal domes collecting wireless electricity output). Features: perimeter wall, science labs, housing unit with dining hall and bathrooms, landing zone, east watchtower. Survived the Svixen attack largely intact.

locations.name: Research Base Bravo
locations.description: Built around a 50-meter Transync Station obelisk — the series' primary Transync hub. Ovular perimeter wall (~250m end to end), center and end sentry towers. Hollow interior chamber with a false door at ground level that becomes a shimmering blue liquid portal when active. Becomes the crew's permanent base of operations after the Svixen attack. Josh sets up a workshop inside the obelisk. Salvaged Dispense-All and power cubes provide food and energy. The Maaket enter through the activated Transync portal here, abducting five crew members.

locations.name: The Mining Cave (Emergency Hideout)
locations.description: An abandoned strip-mine 1,920km northeast of the FOB. Three horizontal shelves carved into a mountainside, each 30m+ tall, with a large internal cavern big enough to conceal two LOLAs. Used by the 15 survivors after the Svixen attack — LOLA One was parked behind a massive fallen boulder inside, LOLA Two behind a printed LSPD wall across the cave mouth. Abandoned when the crew moves permanently to Research Base Bravo.

locations.name: Tual
locations.description: The homeworld of the Maaket — stealthy, feline-like warriors with advanced technology and a fierce warrior culture. Connected to the Transync Network with multiple stations. Architecture blends medieval European forms with advanced technology: polished stone arches etched with glowing glyphs, energy barrier security systems, hovering utility carts, and silent atmospheric craft. The royal palace contains a vaulted throne room, stained glass, feline-motif banners, and holding cells with active false-image barriers. Josh remains on Tual for a period after the crew departs, working alongside Maaket engineers to develop new technologies — including the Maaket Braid Device — that are delivered to the crew aboard the Ascendant. A key ally in the conflict against the Svixen, offering resources, technology, and personnel.

locations.name: Seutor
locations.description: The homeworld of the Tuaca and Natuul — an ancient, jungle and canyon-riddled world. Dense jungle with violet-tinged nights and pervasive bioluminescence: orange rhythmic flickers at ground level, teal tendrils swaying in the canopy. Plants appear to share biological signals, brightening and dimming in response to nearby activity. Multiple Transync Stations across the planet, most damaged in Natuul/Svixen attacks. Home to the Lesfer Spaceport City, built into a multi-tiered stepped canyon. It is on Seutor that the Ascendant is obtained, and where Chris wins a Skrema duel to save the world. A place of alliance, sacrifice, farewell, and hope.

locations.name: Calores Region (Seutor)
locations.description: An urban region on Seutor under the stewardship of Patron Rohga. Site of a Tuaca safehouse network built into ancient cave systems. After a Natuul/Svixen attack, most of the population retreats underground. The local Transync Station survived the attack and was used by Chris's team for outbound travel. Rohga maintains a scouting party at the station to relay communications to any arriving crew from Catora.

locations.name: Lesfer Spaceport City
locations.description: A Tuaca-controlled spaceport city built into a multi-tiered stepped canyon on Seutor. The canyon dwarfs the Grand Canyon in scale. A winding road carved into sheer cliff faces connects the jungle plateau above to the city and spaceport below. The aesthetic mixes steampunk-industrial architecture with old-western layout and underground ship storage via elevator lifts. Multiple landing pads are heavily damaged from the Natuul/Svixen attack.
