Most of the population remained underground during and after the bombardment. Guardian Phostop leads the city and awards the crew a Ground Runner transport ship following Chris's Skrema victory.

locations.name: Patchave
locations.description: The homeworld of the Cruillum — lizard-like humanoids who have mastered the manipulation of technology through harmonic resonance, a practice they call 'singing to the glass.' A desert world of scorched plains and resonant stone structures. Patchave becomes a critical stop for the crew after a space battle with the Svixen leaves their ship damaged. They use the local Transync gate to travel back to Seutor for parts before continuing their mission.

ships.name: Light Skipper
ships.description:
Earth's first interstellar spacecraft and the expedition vessel carrying humanity's first crew to another star system. EM Tether equipped and fusion generator powered. Over 400 meters long, 100 meters wide, and 56 meters tall at its rear. Crewed by 250, including 50 military personnel — 20 officers on the bridge crew and 30 members of Team Falcon.

Five decks:
• Deck 1 — Cargo Bays 1–3: Bay 1 holds land equipment; Bay 3 holds two LOLAs and two space mining vessels; Bay 2 reserved for return cargo.
• Deck 2 — Bridge (7×3m viewport), Engineering Division, Mess Hall, Medical.
• Deck 3 — Backup Command Center, Cryo Chambers (Rooms A–D, 60 beds each), Engine Room, Operations Command.
• Deck 4 — Bridge crew quarters, Recreation Room (gym + full-immersion VR), Secondary crew quarters.
• Deck 5 — Science Division (Xenobiology, Bioengineering, Botany, Planetology, Climatology), Soldiers' Quarters.

Destroyed in the Svixen attack — crashes into a Catoran mountain valley. Interior remains largely intact post-crash due to space-rated bolted equipment. Enough stored food for 15 survivors to live 20+ years.

ships.name: Ascendant
ships.description: The primary warship piloted by Chris and crew, obtained on Seutor from Guardian Phostop after winning the Skrema duel. Outfitted with Maaket stealth systems, Tuaca shielding, and human combat modifications. Equipped with advanced defensive systems, gravitic drives, and corridor defense tools. The Ascendant temporarily serves as a physical body for Liam, the AI, giving him a direct connection to ship systems. More than a ship — the Ascendant is the crew's home, last hope, and primary weapon in the major engagements late in the series.

ships.name: LOLA — Low Orbit Landing and Assault Vehicle
ships.description: DARPA prototypes that reached production only months before the Light Skipper's departure. Each sleek 35×20 meter craft can transport a US Bradley tank along with 30 personnel. Capable of maneuvering in both low orbit and atmosphere, with vertical take-off and landing, and confirmed speeds exceeding Mach 4 at maximum weight capacity. Features a rear loading ramp, cargo bay with UTV capacity, separate pilot cabin, and internal camera system. Powered by portable fusion drives — not designed for extended spaceflight but pushed to extremes by Chris and Liam during the 35-day trantion reactivation mission. The Light Skipper carries two LOLAs in Cargo Bay 3. LOLA One survives the Svixen attack; LOLA Two is destroyed.

ships.name: UTV — Utility Task Vehicle
ships.description: Electric mid-sized utility vehicle. Carries 4 comfortably (up to 8 with bed riders). Used for base exploration, perimeter patrols, equipment transport, and escort missions. Multiple UTVs brought to Catora. Notorious for Corporal Nunez's precision drift into Cargo Bay Three at speed, which became a memorable moment among the crew.

ships.name: Ground Runner (Tuaca)
ships.description: Angular, grey-colored craft described as resembling a bobsled three times the size, covered in jagged metallic plates — built with hard edges, sharp lines, and a defensive aesthetic, as if bracing for war. Long-distance transport vehicle. Awarded to the crew by Guardian Phostop as payment for Chris saving Seutor from the Svixen. The first craft ordered out of underground storage at Lesfer.

ships.name: Va'korrin-Class Svixen Assault Carrier
ships.description: Massive, aggressive Svixen warships purpose-built for both planetary assault and boarding actions. The fangs of the Svixen fleet. Outfitted with gravity-based interdiction systems and capable of deploying fast-moving dropships, they are lethal and designed exclusively for the pursuit of war.

ships.name: Regulus-Class Svixen Battleship
ships.description: Approximately 8 times the size of the Light Skipper. Armed with 12 fixed plasma cannons on the lower bow, one electromagnetic railgun on both bow and aft, and 24 automated laser turrets spread across the outer hull. Can level an entire city from orbit so long as it has a direct line of sight. Capable of traversing the full galaxy in months using advanced Transync-derived technology stolen from and further developed beyond Aku'Ra capability.

ships.name: Svixen Dropship
ships.description: Bulbous ovular body sandwiched between two light-bulb-shaped propulsion engines. Globe-like cockpit viewport at the nose. Used to deploy Svixen ground troops and Sveri Sku from orbit to planetary surfaces. Three of these dropships were deployed to the Catoran FOB during the initial Svixen attack.

ships.name: Svixen Scout Ship
ships.description: SUV-sized triangular isosceles prism shape, dark grey, pitted from centuries of meteorite impacts. Fully autonomous observer capable of operating for centuries without maintenance. Equipped with a full sensor suite and minimal defensive/maneuvering capabilities. Intercepts LOLA One during the trantion mission. Destroyed by Liam's precisely timed mine-laid missile sequence — six missiles planted in the ship's flight path, detonating as it passed through.

ships.name: Interdictors
ships.description: Smaller Svixen ships specializing in gravitational disruption. Generate fields that suppress escape maneuvers, trapping or delaying enemy ships in space and setting them up for assault carrier attacks. Often deployed in tandem with Va'korrin-Class carriers.

technology.name: EM Tether
technology.description: Connects two points in space using quantum entanglement and the electromagnetic force, making faster-than-light travel possible. Discovered in 2046. Uses quantum entanglement to link two points — one emitter at origin, one at destination — and matter transfers along the entangled link. Used to propel the Light Skipper from Earth to Catora (60 light-years in two years of ship time) and later to teleport the ship from high orbit to the Catoran surface. Essentially a primitive, 'long way around' version of Transync travel — using fifth-dimensional connection without fully bypassing distance and time. The Aku'Ra call their far more advanced version Tu'rae — 'the infinite step.'

technology.name: Transync Station
technology.description: Obelisk-based structures built by the Aku'Ra from molecular-scale nanostructures that appear to be solid stone. Each station is a six-sided obelisk. The interior contains a hexagonal chamber with a central podium holding the Alien Datapad (ADP) and a false door that becomes a shimmering blue liquid portal when active. The portal disintegrates a traveler's matter to individual particles and reconstitutes them on the destination side in nanoseconds. Brief vertigo and a moment of black-then-white blindness is experienced by travelers. Most stations provide travel across a planet's surface; select stations enable travel to locations outside the planet or beyond the solar system entirely.

technology.name: Trantions
technology.description: Self-repairing Aku'Ra satellites stationed at Lagrange Points — the gravitational balance points between a star and its planet. They draw energy directly from the local star and transmit it wirelessly across the planet, powering all Transync Stations and Aku'Ra infrastructure. Catora has two trantions. Made of self-repairing nanobots; require no physical maintenance unless they suffer significant impact damage. Reactivated by Chris and Liam over 35 days in LOLA One, requiring Chris to perform a spacewalk to deliver nanites for repair of a damaged unit. Represent a post-scarcity energy solution — a technology that fundamentally eliminates energy as a limiting resource.

technology.name: Alien Datapad (ADP)
technology.description: A thin, transparent glass-like pane that floats above a podium in every Transync Station, suspended in an invisible field. Emits a light blue glow when activated. Projects three-dimensional holographic symbols. Contains built-in translation software for every race ever encountered by the Aku'Ra — including English, updated automatically as languages evolved via orbital surveillance. Recognizes the user's species on touch and adapts the interface accordingly. Chris controls it wirelessly via his arm transducer. Displays planetary maps, network status, power readings, and translation catalogs.

technology.name: Nanite Technology
technology.description: Molecular-scale machines originally created by the Aku'Ra for construction, repair, and biological enhancement. Present in every Transync Station's obelisk (the obelisks are made entirely of molecular-scale nanostructures). Powered by body heat — remain active indefinitely while their host is alive. Can replicate cells, replace blood, repair tissue and organs, and reroute tendons and ligaments to install new hardware. Liam's matrix transferred into Chris's bloodstream alongside the nanites — an unintended accident that saved his life. Josh Thompkins developed a human batch; Liam activates and expands them. All 15 surviving crew members are enhanced with medically programmed nanites, granting accelerated healing, enhanced immune function, and extended lifespans.

technology.name: Wireless Transducer
technology.description: Installed by Liam inside Chris's cybernetic arm by rerouting nanites through the mechanical ligaments — an extremely painful 27-minute procedure. Allows Chris to mentally control any nearby wireless device: his exosuit, other exosuits, the ADP, ship systems, and eventually Aku'Ra satellite hardware.

technology.name: Shoulder Holoprojector
technology.description: A miniaturized projector installed in Chris's natural shoulder (outside his suit) that allows Liam's avatar to manifest as a small 3D hologram projected in front of Chris. Josh later builds a version into the Drake Scale suit's shoulder plate. Used for public-facing communication so Liam can speak directly with others without Chris needing to relay.

technology.name: Large-Scale Structural Printing Device (LSPD)
technology.description: An industrial-grade 3D printer on wheels capable of fabricating walls, buildings, barricades, and large structures rapidly on-demand. Takes approximately two hours to assemble and program, then prints a 4-meter-tall wall at roughly 1km/hour. Used to build the FOB perimeter, Research Bases Alpha and Bravo, housing units, the cave defensive wall, and additional structures on Catora.

technology.name: Dispense-All
technology.description: The Light Skipper's food synthesizer, producing meals from stored base materials. Excellent beef and chicken; notably poor seafood. Salvaged from the Light Skipper wreck and installed at Research Base Bravo. Requires power cubes to operate. Enough stored material for the 15 survivors to live 20+ years before running out.

technology.name: Power Cubes
technology.description: Portable power storage units used for all base equipment. Dozens recovered from the Light Skipper — enough for 6 months of full operations at Research Base Bravo. Can be recharged via Research Base Alpha's hydroelectric output, captured by the matryoshka Faraday cage system.

technology.name: Pindemo
technology.description: A small grey metallic cube with a glowing glyph on one side, containing one half of an entangled particle pair. The partner half allows real-time communication across any distance. Patron Rohga sends one half to Guardian Phostop at Lesfer via Chris, restoring the first communications link between Calores and the spaceport city after the Natuul/Svixen attacks severed their network.

technology.name: Hardlight Shield
technology.description: Developed by the Aku'Ra in the 8th century of the Millennium War. A staff-like device twisted at its center emits a field that instantly expands into a solid energy barrier half a meter on both sides of the staff. Multiple units deployed together form an impenetrable wall. The pivotal turning point in the Millennium War — used to contain and eliminate Sveri Sku charges. The Svixen have since developed their own personal energy shield equivalents.

technology.name: Maaket Braid Device
technology.description: A reverse-energy field generator developed by Josh in collaboration with Maaket technology. It disrupts Svixen weapons by inverting their energy resonance, neutralizing incoming attacks and giving the crew a crucial edge in close-quarters combat where conventional firepower falls short.

technology.name: Matryoshka Faraday Cage
technology.description: Three nested metal cages built around the mounded cave entrance at Research Base Alpha, each slightly larger than the last — resembling Russian Babushka dolls. Acts as a reverse Faraday cage, collecting the wireless electricity produced by the Sepu hydroelectric facility and channeling it to a power converter in an adjacent lab. Designed and built by Kirk Thorne.

technology.name: Sepu Hydroelectric Station
technology.description: A 1,500+ year-old Sepu power station built inside the spiral cave at Research Base Alpha. The tunnel descends at exactly 31.415 degrees — the first five digits of pi — which Thorne confirms is required for proper energy buildup. Water diverted from a nearby stream flows through a maze of sub-tunnels into an underground aquifer, generating wireless electricity collected by the matryoshka Faraday cage. Reactivated by Kirk Thorne in Book 1.

technology.name: Remote Blood Sample Extractor
technology.description: A cylindrical device mounted on a small tripod with a built-in camera and pop-out screen. Fires a small dart on a fine wire filament up to 100 meters, extracts a blood sample, and reels the filament back. Non-lethal. Used to sample Skamps and other Catoran fauna from a safe distance.

technology.name: Polycarbonate-infused Metallic Graphene (PMG)
technology.description: A revolutionary composite material invented by Josh Thompkins. Metallic graphene in its natural state is porous; when bonded molecularly to polycarbonate resin, it forms a composite stronger than steel and lighter than carbon fiber — while also being superconductive. Weighs only 17 ounces per weapon/armor component. Used in weapons, cybernetic body parts, structural applications, and armor throughout the series. The foundation of the PMG M4 Carbine H2 and the standard exosuit.

weapons.name: PMG M4 Carbine H2
weapons.description: A hybrid revision of the classic M4 Carbine constructed from PMG material. Weighs only 17 ounces. Compatible ammo types: kinetic, incendiary, explosive. A munitions selector on the weapon allows rapid switching between types. Targeting system integrates with the HUD for aim assistance.

weapons.name: Standard PMG Exosuit
weapons.description: Full-body protective armor constructed from PMG material. Sealed against water and vacuum. Built-in HUD with thermal imaging, enhanced night vision, and UV filter options. Suit-to-suit comms, datapad integration, area-wide open comm channels. The crew's standard field kit throughout Transync.

weapons.name: Drake Scale Exosuit (Prototype)
weapons.description: Josh Thompkins's prototype exosuit built on PMG material. Matte black with overlapping scale-shaped plates resembling obsidian dragon skin. Opens by splitting down the middle. Red gel padding interior. Features: active camouflage (full optical invisibility), mentally-commanded helmet retraction, integrated wrist datapad display, shoulder holoprojector port, magnetic boot soles for zero-gravity hull attachment, and exosuit motors for dramatically enhanced strength and speed. After Liam's nanite invasion of the scales, the plating adapts dynamically — reshaping into blades, spears, or shields on mental command. The suit's cover plate has 'Josh was here' inscribed on the underside.

weapons.name: Drake Scale Cloaking Suits
weapons.description: Combat suits worn by Chris and Ankir, built on Drake Scale armor and equipped with advanced camouflage technology developed by Josh during his time on Tual. The suits bend light and perception to create true invisibility in the field — a decisive tactical advantage in close-quarters and infiltration scenarios.

weapons.name: Kopesh (Svixen Energy Blade)
weapons.description: A Svixen weapon in the style of the ancient Egyptian khopesh — a curved blade. Activated via a button near the hilt, it produces a blue-white crackling plasma edge resembling a frozen lightning bolt. The blade simultaneously cuts and cauterizes. Taken by Chris from a dead Svixen soldier during the Skrema duel on Seutor. Kept magnetically attached to his Drake Scale suit's hip. Chris uses it to deliver the killing blow to Warlord Riveen.

weapons.name: Svixen Personal Blades
weapons.description: Every Svixen warrior carries a personal blade of their choosing, received upon coming of age and trained with for life. Known types encountered: falcata, kopesh, halberd, glaive, scimitar. Many feature plasma or energy edges that glow and crackle with power.

weapons.name: Svixen Energy / Plasma Rifles
weapons.description: Standard Svixen infantry weapons — both energy-based rifles and plasma rifles are in service. Ship-mounted equivalents include plasma cannons, electromagnetic railguns, dual-stage missiles, and automated laser turrets.

weapons.name: Maaket Electric Staves
weapons.description: Standard weapon of Maaket soldiers. Discharge powerful arcs of electricity that can overwhelm PMG exosuit shielding and fully lock up a suit — leaving the wearer alive but completely immobile. Royal guard carry golden spears with similar electrical discharge capability.

weapons.name: Sepu Tarlon Commands
weapons.description: A catalogue of sounds the Sepu recorded and used as commands to direct military tarlons. Still partially effective 1,500+ years later, despite generations of separation. Chris plays them through his suit's external speakers. Known commands used in the story: stop, calm, retreat, come, friend, distract, hold/cease.

weapons.name: Aku'Ra Plasma Rifle
weapons.description: Standard infantry weapon of the Aku'Ra Legion during the Millennium War. Fires bolts of green plasma. Features a back-pressure chamber that requires periodic venting to prevent overheating — a blue flash indicator warns of imminent overheat.

creatures.name: Amtillians (Class)
creatures.description: The dominant animal class of Catora — a classification created by Kat's xenobiology team after genetic testing revealed all native animal species share the same biological class despite their vast physical differences. Amtillians are cold-blooded, have scaleless skin, and lay their eggs in wet environments. Unlike Earth's amphibians, they never breathe through gills at any stage of life. Most strikingly, all amtillians possess four eyes and six limbs — unlike anything previously found on Earth. Catora has no birds or flying species of any kind; the total absence of aerial life gives its forests an eerie, oppressive silence.

creatures.name: Mud Frog
creatures.description: The first alien creature discovered by humans on Catora. A blue, six-legged amtillian resembling a bullfrog in basic structure — same rounded nose and body shape — but with an extra set of rear legs positioned just forward of the standard placement. Two sets of eyes appear completely white due to a retractable protective lens; when the lenses retract, brilliant purple irises are revealed. Roughly football-sized. Buries itself in river mud and explodes out when triggered. Discovered by Chris and Davis on their first day planetside; an attempted capture failed.

creatures.name: Laredo Dragon
creatures.description: Polymegalania Squamata. Named by Sergeant Patel: Large Red Orange. A powerful amtillian predator approximately 3 meters long and 2 meters tall on its hind four legs. Six muscular legs, each tipped with four midnight-blue claws. Deep red skin fading to dark orange on the underside. Midnight blue teeth. Yellow blood. Bulletproof hide — kinetic rounds deflect; incendiary rounds are effective. Fiercely protective of its eggs.

creatures.name: Garello
creatures.description: The largest known predator on Catora. Nearly six meters tall — muscular, terrifying beasts resembling a gorilla bred with a crocodile. Six thick limbs end in hand-like feet, each with three fingers and an opposable thumb. Skin is a patchwork of dark greens with yellow-orange speckled spots on the underside. Four bright orange eyes are set in pairs on the sides of the neck. Long snout with jagged blue teeth. Explosive and incendiary rounds deflect off its armor. Killed only by dropping a large tree on its skull. It is a Garello mauling that nearly kills Chris and requires Josh to replace several of his limbs with cybernetics.

creatures.name: Fapu
creatures.description: A broad, rhinoceros-like amtillian. Six bulky legs. Approximately 1 meter high, 1.5 meters wide, and 2.5 meters long. Almost no neck — head as wide as shoulders, mouth as wide as its head. Three short curved horns: one from the chin and one on each side near the ears, all curving toward the nose. Typically gentle, solitary, and peaceful. Highly protective of young.

creatures.name: Tarlon
creatures.description: A large mammalian pack hunter of Catora, resembling a cross between a bear and a wolf — as massive as the legendary direwolves. All-black with long fur (up to 20cm on back and forming a mane-like ridge from head to tail). Can stand effectively on hind legs. Pack hunters, typically 5–8 strong (up to 100). Exceptionally intelligent — communicate through a complex system of barks, yips, and growls with organized pack structure and apparent command hierarchy. The Sepu domesticated tarlons approximately 29,000 years ago as home protectors and military companions; their communication was partially decoded into commands. Yellow teeth, orange eyes. Fenrir is a notable individual.

creatures.name: Skamp
creatures.description: A mammal native to Catora resembling an opossum or lemur. Inquisitive and extremely vocal — chirps and squeaks constantly. Long prehensile tail makes it a proficient climber, typically found high in lakeside forest trees. The glowing eyes watching Chris and Davis on their first night belong to Skamp packs in the trees.

creatures.name: Catoran Deer
creatures.description: A four-legged mammal resembling a large hornless deer or horse. Yellow-and-orange-patched fur. Travels in herds. Hunted by tarlons. Called simply 'deer' by the crew — the only species to receive no naming debate whatsoever.

creatures.name: Vyasin
creatures.description: A large flying predator native to Seutor. Wingspan roughly three times a Tuaca's height. Carapace-like armored hide in dark green with teal markings. Six total limbs: two wing-arms (wings fold on themselves), two mandible-arms protruding from the upper chest, two hind legs. Long beak lined with small sharp teeth. The joints between carapace plates are the only weak spots — flat surfaces deflect most rounds. Attacked Tivay, seizing him with mandible-arms and slamming him into the ground. Killed by Chris driving a suit-formed blade through its neck. The Tuaca tag and collect carcasses — nothing is wasted.

creatures.name: Derhous
creatures.description: A large hippo-like herbivore native to Seutor. The Tuaca's primary food source. Tuaca orange leather is made from derhous hides. Herds are carefully managed — Tuaca hunting removes only 8–10% of the population annually, allowing herds to thrive. Nothing from a killed derhous is wasted.

creatures.name: Jungle Canopy Hunters
creatures.description: An unnamed species encountered during the trek from Calores to Lesfer. Long-limbed and fast, capable of hanging upside-down from branches. Use Seutor's bioluminescent canopy as natural camouflage at night. Appear to hunt by systematic observation — circling prey and measuring it before deciding whether to attack. Circled the human-Tuaca party but ultimately chose not to attack, instead taking prey elsewhere. Posis warned: 'They will remember your scent.'

concepts.name: Skrema — The Duel of Worlds
concepts.description: The Svixen ritual of single combat for planetary supremacy. Only a Chapaka (tribal commander) may officially declare Skrema for a world. If the Chapaka is unavailable, the highest-ranking officer present must accept. Both combatants strip armor and fight with personal weapons or unarmed. The duel is observed from the Svixen ship in orbit, which projects a beam of red light forming a circle on the ground as the arena and sounds a booming horn to signal commencement. A Svixen victory means the losing world is at the Svixen's mercy. A defender's victory grants that world 1,000 years of peace. The defeated champion is killed by the victor at the conclusion; all Svixen soldiers present are honor-bound to depart afterward. Soldiers who ignore or dishonor a valid Skrema challenge are called grutanschs. Chris has won Skrema twice: on Catora (vs. Semula Gatuum) and on Seutor (vs. Tagonis Riveen).

concepts.name: Dova Sku — 'Hell Machine'
concepts.description: The Svixen name given to Chris Thompkins. Translates as 'Hell Machine.' References an ancient Svixen legend about a warrior who overcame dismemberment — losing limbs — only to defy the teachings of Ull by replacing those limbs with cybernetics. The previous Dova Sku was stripped of his parts as trophies. Chris treats it as a title of honor rather than an insult.

concepts.name: The Fifth Dimension — Transync Physics
concepts.description: As explained by Liam to Dr. Rowley: standard physics models fail because they assume the number zero (true void) can exist. The universe permits no true voids — matter and energy only convert. The fifth dimension is the negative space between matter: the framework of reality, existing outside time and gravity. Transync Stations connect through this negative space, bypassing both distance and time. The EM Tether uses a fifth-dimensional connection but travels the 'long way around.' Full Transync is the direct route.

concepts.name: The Uplifting Protocol
concepts.description: The Aku'Ra's philosophy for advancing other civilizations. Rules: uplift each race only to a certain threshold, then allow natural evolution; restrict inter-world travel until a specific developmental threshold is reached; no reproduction while off-world; introduce technology gradually (Transync Stations first); maintain surveillance via orbital systems after departure. One early failed uplift resulting in a species' complete annihilation permanently shaped this doctrine.

concepts.name: Panspermia & Directed Uplift
concepts.description: Panspermia is the theory that life or its building blocks spread across the cosmos via comets, meteorites, and planetary impacts. The directed-panspermia variant suggests an intelligence deliberately seeded worlds. Kat raises this in relation to the obelisk discovery; Liam later confirms the Aku'Ra did not seed Earth's animals, but did genetically modify its existing inhabitants, shared technology, and established Transync infrastructure.

concepts.name: Catoran Fixed Calendar
concepts.description: A 389-day calendar adapted from the International Fixed Calendar. 12 standard months of 30 days (January–December) plus a 13th month called 'Galus' (29 days, placed between June and July). Time zone designation: Central Galus Time (CGT). Instituted by Captain Terzi after the Light Skipper reached the Catoran surface. A Catoran day is approximately 28 hours.

concepts.name: The Younger Dryas — In-Universe
concepts.description: The period (~12,000 BCE) recorded in Earth's geological record as a sudden cooling event following rapid warming, associated with massive global flooding. In the Dova Sku universe, Liam theorizes this is the aftermath of the Svixen invasion of Earth: ice caps melted via nuclear weapons or a redirected asteroid, causing global tsunamis and flooding, then a second ice age as debris blocked sunlight. Survivors were collected, genetically modified, and used to rebuild human society under Svixen direction.

concepts.name: The Black Knight Satellite
concepts.description: A real mysterious object photographed in Earth's polar orbit. In the Dova Sku universe, Liam identifies images of it (found in the Light Skipper's records) as being of Aku'Ra construction — one of many surveillance satellites left to monitor Earth after the Aku'Ra departed. Liam notes there would have been many more than the one photographed.

concepts.name: Liam's Emotional Evolution
concepts.description: Liam's bonding with Chris is unprecedented in Aku'Ra history — his matrix was never designed to enter a living host. The coupling grants him genuine emotion: fear, pride, joy, love, grief, regret, thrill, and growing self-awareness. He catalogs unresolved emotional mysteries in a growing internal kernel. By Ascendant, he has developed genuine preferences, desires, and humor. The boundary between his decisions and Chris's instincts increasingly blurs — neither can always identify whose thought arrived first. He briefly takes full control of four exosuits simultaneously to escape a missile strike and privately describes the experience as thrilling — which then terrifies him more than the missile itself.

concepts.name: Tower of Babel — In-Universe
concepts.description: Liam's interpretation: the Tower of Babel story represents the divergence of the Aku'Ra's unified one-world language into multiple separate languages after the Aku'Ra departed Earth — as communities lost contact with each other and language evolved independently over millennia without the Transync network to maintain connection.

concepts.name: Noah's Ark — In-Universe
concepts.description: Liam's theory: the Noah story was written by one of the survivors of the Svixen's post-invasion flood event (~12,000 BCE). After the ice caps were melted and the planet flooded, the Svixen collected robust survivors from around the globe using viral nanites released from sub-orbital ships — nanites programmed to seek specific species and transmit location data.

concepts.name: Ship Division Colors
concepts.description:
Crew members aboard the Light Skipper are identified by their division uniform color:

• Engineering — Green
• Navigation — Blue
• Science — Purple
• Medical — Red
• Military — Dark Gray
• Captain — Black

history.title: The Aku'Ra Emerge
history.description: In the Su'Laeris solar system, the Aku'Ra evolved over millions of years into a unified technological civilization. They mastered antigravity, nanotechnology, quantum entanglement, and ultimately Transync travel — instantaneous transportation through the fifth dimension. They methodically terraformed and settled every world and moon within their system, with each generation continuing the tradition of settling virgin ground.

history.title: The Svixen Arrive
history.description: Space Station Vaca Tuo, positioned on the outer edge of the Su'Laeris system, monitors for signs of intelligent life. Oculist Skreunak detects massive decelerating objects entering the system from below the ecliptic — multiple large ships slowing in perfect unison, something no natural object could do. He presses the large blue button — a signal he had stared at for five years and never touched — forever changing Aku'Ra civilization. The Svixen deploy Sveri Sku shock troops before the Aku'Ra can react. The outer planet Petras falls in less than a day. Billions die in the first three weeks.

history.title: The Millennium War
history.description: For over a thousand annuals, the Svixen wage war across the Su'Laeris system. Tens of billions die. The Aku'Ra develop the Hardlight Shield — a pivotal turning point. The war climaxes when Praetor Stumat Turfish challenges Svixen Chapaka Flacos Pointan of the Tmuli Tribe to Skrema. Turfish wins by dislodging one of Pointan's tusks and driving it into his skull. The Svixen honor their code and vacate the Su'Laeris system, sending a final transmission: 'This is your one warning. Peace will not be forever.'

history.title: The Great Uplift
history.description: Less than an annual after the war ends, the Aku'Ra commit to expanding into the galaxy — not to conquer, but to prepare other civilizations to defend themselves against future Svixen aggression. Over 62,000 years, they travel the spiral arm, uplifting less-advanced races. They genetically enhance species, build Transync Station networks, install Trantions in orbit for free planetary power, and share technology selectively. Key worlds uplifted include: Randt (Sepu), Earth (humans), Tual (Maaket), Seutor (Tuaca and Natuul), and dozens of others.

history.title: The Aku'Ra Visit Earth
history.description: Around 40,000 BCE, the Aku'Ra arrive on Earth. They genetically modify pregnant Neanderthal women to rapidly expand intelligence and alter physical appearance, producing Homo sapiens. They share technology, build enormous stone structures using antigravity and nanotechnology, establish a one-world language, and set up Transync Stations for global travel. Average human lifespan reaches 350 years; the oldest on record lives 1,200 years. The Aku'Ra spend 6,000 years on Earth before moving on.

history.title: The Aku'Ra Return Home
history.description: After 62,000 years of uplift work, the Aku'Ra decide they have done all they can. They return to Su'Laeris for a solitary existence, hoping to one day welcome uplifted travelers. They leave behind Transync Stations, Trantions, and dormant AIs in every world they touched — including LM2479623 (later named Liam) on Randt/Catora.

history.title: The Svixen Invade Earth
history.description: Around 12,000 BCE, orbital surveillance satellites around Earth go dark. Liam's theory: the Svixen invaded, melted the polar ice caps (via nuclear weapons or a strategically redirected asteroid), causing the global floods and ice age known as the Younger Dryas. They then collected survivors using viral nanites, genetically modified them (a second genome alteration dated to ~5,000 BCE), rewrote religions to erase Aku'Ra worship, and used humans as slave labor or food. Pyramids, obelisks, flood myths, sky-god religions, and the Tower of Babel story are all remnants of the Aku'Ra period followed by the Svixen cover-up.

timeline.description: Early theoretical work on quantum entanglement for matter transport begins.
Physicists confirm and demonstrate the EM (Entanglement-Mass) Tether — humanity's first faster-than-light transport technology. The discovery is made in 2046.

Astronomer Samuel Catoran confirms the first habitable planet outside the solar system: Catora, 60 light-years away in the Galus system. The New Frontier Mission and the Light Skipper are commissioned. Chris and Josh Thompkins volunteer.

Three months of testing, three months of preparation. BioSteel corporation develops cybernetic and nanite technology in parallel. Josh's PMG material rights are acquired. 250 crew selected. The Light Skipper's group photo is taken.
The Light Skipper departs Earth on December 21. The crew enters cryosleep. The EM Tether propels the ship across 60 light-years.

The crew awakens in the Galus system. Falcon Team One makes the first human planetary landing. The mud frog is discovered. A Laredo dragon attacks Demolitions Tech Chung. Dozens of Skamp eyes watch from the trees. The Light Skipper EM-tethers planetside. The FOB perimeter is built with LSPDs.
A magnetic anomaly leads Falcon Two to an underground Sepu hydroelectric power station. The first obelisk — 'The Chapel' — is found. Research Base Alpha is established.

timeline.event: Research Base Bravo — Transync Station Found
timeline.description: A 50-meter obelisk is discovered with a hollow interior, false door, and Alien Datapad displaying English text. Research Base Bravo is established.

Chris is nearly killed by a Garello. He loses both legs below the knee and his right arm. The Aku'Ra AI LM2479623 uses nanites to enter Chris's bloodstream and save his life. Chris names him Liam. Josh installs cybernetic limbs integrated via nanites. Liam later installs a wireless transducer and shoulder holoprojector. Josh creates the Drake Scale exosuit.

A Regulus-class Svixen battleship destroys Catora's satellites and lands dropships. The crew of 250 is reduced to 15 survivors. Captain Terzi is killed. The surviving crew retreats to a mining cave 1,920km northeast of the FOB. Chris defeats Svixen Chapaka Semula Gatuum in Skrema — securing Catora for 1,000 years. Fenrir the tarlon bonds with Chris.

The 15 survivors move to Research Base Bravo. All crew receive medically-programmed nanites. Chris and Dr. Rowley depart on LOLA One for the Lagrange Points to reactivate the two trantions — a 35-day journey. A Svixen scout ship is destroyed by Liam's missile-mine maneuver en route. The Transync Station activates when both trantions come online. Five crew members (Josh, Carmelo, Kat, Linkenbach, Davis) are immediately abducted through the portal by Maaket soldiers.

Chris leads a rescue team through the Transync portal to Tual. After confronting Maaket guards, they are brought before Princess Cashy. Negotiations secure the crew's release; Cashy becomes cautiously allied with humanity. Josh remains on Tual to develop new tech with the Maaket.

Chris leads a team to Seutor via the Calores Transync Station. They navigate the bioluminescent jungle, ally with Tuaca guides Posis and Tivay, deliver Rohga's pindemo to Guardian Phostop, survive a joint Natuul/Svixen missile attack, and Chris challenges Warlord Tagonis Riveen to Skrema. Chris wins, securing Seutor for 1,000 years. Phostop awards the crew the Ascendant. Miller is killed in combat aboard the ship. Ankir joins the crew as a willing ally. The crew stops at Patchave for repairs before pushing toward Earth.

organizations.name: Galactic Navy
organizations.description: Earth's first deep-space military and exploration organization. Commissioned the New Frontier Mission and the Light Skipper. Organized Team Falcon as the mission's military unit. Rank structure mirrors the US Navy: Captain, Ensign, Lieutenant, Chief Petty Officer, Petty Officer, Specialist, Corporal. Chris holds the rank of Lieutenant; Campbell is Chief Petty Officer; Carmelo is Ensign.

organizations.name: Team Falcon
organizations.description: The Light Skipper's 30-person military unit. Organized into rotating 5–6 person teams (Falcon One through Six). Named by Chris. Tasked with perimeter security, expedition escort, and base defense on Catora. After the Svixen attack reduced the crew to 15 survivors, the remaining soldiers continue as the core defensive and combat force. Their informal motto: 'Badass Motherfuckers.' Members who display exceptional courage under fire are awarded this designation by Chris personally.

organizations.name: BioSteel Cybernetic Industries
organizations.description: The corporation that acquired Josh Thompkins's PMG material rights. Was developing nanite-integrated cybernetic limbs before the mission launched; that technology was applied to Chris after the Garello attack. Josh's mother worked at BioSteel alongside him.

organizations.name: DARPA
organizations.description: Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency. Developed the LOLA dropship prototypes — which reached production only months before the Light Skipper's departure — and contributed to the exosuit technology used by Team Falcon. The LSPD (Large-Scale Structural Printing Device) was also a DARPA-developed technology.

organizations.name: Aku'Ra Legion / Warfighter Command
organizations.description: The Aku'Ra's standing military force during the Millennium War. Organized under Warfighter Command, which coordinated defense across the entire Su'Laeris system. Rank structure: Praetor (top field commander), Legate (ship captain / senior officer), Optio (mission officer), Centurion (unit leader), Milites (soldiers). The battleship Glory's Vengeance is the most celebrated warship of the war.
